programme.title: Diagnosis Murder

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan unexpectedly inherits a winning lottery ticket from a dying man - whose widow is understandably not slow in coming forward to claim the multimillion-dollar jackpot. Ken Kercheval (Dallas) guest stars.(n)

programme.title: Father Dowling Mysteries
programme.desc: An angel approaches Steve and Frank with a warning about a parishioner's underworld links. Tom Bosley and Tracy Nelson star.(n)

programme.title: Perry Mason

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: CJ races to the rescue when Walker and Trivette are kidnapped by a gang of criminals.(n)

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: In the first part of a two-part crossover episode with CSI:NY, a prisoner from New York escapes from a crashed plane and goes on a killing spree. As the team tries to locate him, it becomes apparent he may be trying to prove he was not responsible for one of the murders of which he stands accused, prompting Horatio to call in Manhattan CSI Mac Taylor, who worked on the initial investigation. Gary Sinise guest stars.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team tries to track down wanted terrorist Harper Dearing before another US Navy ship is destroyed, and a desperate Gibbs enlists the aid of a prisoner to draw his target out of hiding. Meanwhile, Palmer is forced to consider putting his nuptials on hold as the stand-off with Dearing becomes heated. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A naval officer involved in an illegal underground street race is killed when his vehicle explodes. The team's investigations reveal he was carrying classified equipment in relation to his work on a new form of battery that the military had hoped to adopt, and that the explosion - captured on CCTV - is not in accordance with that expected of normal fuel. Meanwhile, Callen latest driving misdemeanour means he must attend traffic school. Chris O'Donnell stars.(n)

programme.title: Father Dowling Mysteries
programme.desc: Frank gets caught up in the middle of a top-secret FBI case while trying to clear a member of his parish who has been accused of stealing confidential documents. Tom Bosley and Tracy Nelson star.(n)

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.sub-title: The Case of the Envious Editor
programme.desc: Perry defends the beautiful wife of a respected magazine editor when she is accused of murdering her husband's ruthless publisher who was blackmailing her.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: US Navy lieutenant Paul Booth is shot dead while sitting on a park bench. It turns out that the last people he called were his boss, his wife and Penelope Langston - a rebellious academic who also happens to be McGee's grandmother. She says that the victim was an old student of hers who kept in touch, but later reveals she was recruited to work on a special project during the Vietnam War by the head of a top-secret think tank Booth was a member of. Guest starring Lily Tomlin, with Mark Harmon and Sean Murray.(n)

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Part one of two. A legendary Texas Ranger is back to seek justice for his murdered son, and to help track down an assassin. Chuck Norris stars.(n)

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Danny and Aiden investigate the case of employees of a Brooklyn cafe found dead with plastic bags over their heads. As events unfold, a discovery is made about the type of duct tape used on the victims which could help track down the killer. Meanwhile, Mac and Stella set out to locate the body of a man whose severed lower leg has turned up in an alleyway.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Marine gunnery sergeant Wendell Dobbs is gunned down in a drive-by shooting by assailants wielding AK-47s, and the team suspects the victim may have been involved in illegally importing automatic weapons into the country and supplying them to local gangs. Sam goes undercover to find the truth after learning Dobbs gave up his combat post to mentor his younger brother, who had been hanging out with a gang. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A man who died after being knocked down by a van is revealed to be a professional hitman responsible for at least six contract killings - and his next target was to be a navy lieutenant commander. Abby is delighted at being a match for a teenager who needs a kidney transplant, but she is informed that someone else with very similar DNA has also been identified as suitable. Has she found a relative she never knew about?(n)

programme.desc: US Navy captain Jake Marsden is murdered in his hotel room and inquiries lead to marine lieutenant Emma Reynolds - who tells the team she was the intended target. programme.desc: A Navy captain wearing a colourful costume underneath his uniform is shot and killed in a flower shop, and further investigation reveals he was leading a double life as a masked vigilante. Vance brings in Tony's ex-fiancee, journalist Wendy Miller, who claims the victim had been trying to expose a scandal, and as the team pursues the case, a real-estate scam is uncovered. Guest starring Perrey Reeves (Entourage).(n)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team search for a boy from Ralph's class who went missing during a field trip, and Toby gets closer to uncovering the identity of Happy's husband.(n)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team is called to catch thieves who have stolen a rare gem from LA's Natural History Museum, in an attempt extract materials required to enrich a nuclear bomb.(n)

programme.title: The High Chaparral
programme.desc: Billy frees a white girl from the Apaches, only to be taken hostage himself.(n)

programme.title: The High Chaparral
programme.sub-title: Once on a Day in Spring
programme.desc: Victoria returns to her father after a spat with Big John interrupting her father's time with the visiting Countess Maria. Manolito competes for her time while she gives Victoria sage advise on relationships as Big John returns for her. Linda Cristal stars.(n)

programme.title: Bondi Rescue
programme.desc: Two men arrive at the lifeguard tower under the influence of drugs, and one suddenly stops breathing and collapses. Meanwhile, a group of children ignore warnings not to enter the water and must be rescued, and a venomous sea snake is found.(n)

programme.title: Bondi Rescue
programme.desc: Lifeguards are forced to close the beach on Australia Day when fog reduces visibility, but alcohol remains a problem and a man suffers a mysterious wound. Meanwhile, police chase down someone who has attacked a girl.(n)

programme.title: Hoarders: Family Secrets
programme.desc: Two more cases of compulsive hoarders whose habit is affecting their relatives' lives as well as their own. David's three-acre hoard has been deemed a 'blighted property' by the city and is at risk of losing his home. Meanwhile, Nora has been hoarding to deal with the death of her son.(n)

programme.title: Unsolved Mysteries

programme.title: Unsolved Mysteries

programme.title: Judge Judy

Nathan is heartbroken when Candida reveals she plans to tour the world during her gap year and Rita confesses she suspects her husband Martin is having an affair. Kerrie Taylor, Lesley Dunlop and Josie Lawrence star.(n)

programme.desc: As the Gibraltar locals reel from the murder of shipping agent Gordon Fletcher, Brian and Gerry - last seen being carried off in a shipping container - find themselves stranded on the side of a Spanish mountain, and now face the problem of how to get back to the rest of the team. As Sandra attempts to unravel secrets stretching back over three decades and link what has now become three murders, she still has doubts about the innocence of charming entrepreneur Harry Truman - but that doesn't stop her joining him for lunch on his luxury yacht. programme.title: Soldier, Soldier

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Sheelagh finally decides to accept Gabriel's marriage proposal, and Smithy patches up his friendship with Gina, who is feeling more amenable to his overtures now she's been given the cancer all-clear. Meanwhile, Roger agrees to investigate an assault on his estranged wife Sandra's new boyfriend Rick - whose reticence to discuss the incident is soon explained by the discovery that he's a crook.(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Abra tries to help a patient waiting for a transplant by offering to operate with an animal organ, despite the procedure being illegal, and asks Mickie to assist him in theatre. Elliot monitors Margaret's condition as she returns from her stem cell treatment in Singapore, believing her case mirrors that of his wife, Gina.(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: A demonstration turns violent when squatters are evicted from their homes, and Josh and Finlay are forced to enter the fray. Later, they respond to a stabbing incident which places their lives in further danger. Meanwhile, Mel and Penny play Cupid to a cantankerous old man and his worried neighbour. Hospital drama, starring Kwame Kwei-Armah and Ian Bleasdale.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Reg organises a half-centenary celebration of Sun Hill, attended by dignitaries including former DCS Charles Barnett. On a less happy note, Amanda Prosser's insensitive treatment of a bereaved father provokes a hostage crisis, and the real Gabriel Kent turns up out of the blue, spelling big trouble for the unscrupulous copper who has assumed his identity.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: The Fowlers are determined not to rise to the Chapman's bait. Phil is late for Kathy's party and Mandy & Aidan are left out in the cold.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: It's Boxing day and Mandy and Aidan have to face the truth. Gita has some news for Sanjay and Phil is increasingly troubled about Nadia.(n)

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: Carole is left in charge while Nick goes away to a conference, but things do not exactly go as smoothly as she would have hoped. Meanwhile, a new fire-fighter joins the team.(n)

programme.title: Monarch of the Glen
programme.desc: Tree surgeon Malcolm arrives to deal with the forest's blight problem - and Paul is dismayed to discover the newcomer is Iona's ex-fiance. Molly coaches Jess to compete in the ghillies' gruelling annual challenge, despite the fact the other competitors snub her efforts. Meanwhile, Ewan is envious when Amy starts spending a great deal of time with her art tutor. Highland drama, starring Lloyd Owen and Rae Hendrie.(n)

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Beth and Tom prepare to tie the knot, but reckon without a few last-minute hitches as Joe suddenly decides he can't be the best man and the bride worries over whether to invite her father. Meanwhile, Sally bites off more than she can chew, the green-eyed monster in Anna makes an appearance when Beth's cousin takes a shine to David and a multiple sclerosis sufferer requires assistance.(n)

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Darryl plots to spend a night with Tracey by escaping from hospital, while Sharon makes her own plans for the evening's entertainment. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson.(n)

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Boycie uncovers disturbing information regarding his ancestral line. Tyler's surprising return from university adds to his sense of worry, convincing him something serious is about to happen. John Challis and Sue Holderness star.(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: The trio scheme to get Wally out from under Nora Batty's thumb for a day, but come a cropper sneaking him back to the house. Comedy, starring Bill Owen and Joe Gladwin.(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: The trio stumble across a health farm on a day out, leading Foggy to devise an ingenious money-making scam - selling food to its famished inhabitants. Comedy, starring Brian Wilde.(n)

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: A schoolgirl's killing brings Gently into the alien world of pop and media celebrity when it turns out the victim's best friend is a rising TV star. Bacchus suspects the dead girl's music teacher, since rumours persist that she was having an affair with him. But when it seems everyone has a different opinion of the girl, Gently must uncover these different faces to get to the truth of her murder. Neil Morrissey guest stars alongside Martin Shaw and Lee Ingleby.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: While Brian spends time at home making peace with himself over his dismissal, he agrees to help Esther's friend Margaret find her missing brother Peter, in the interest of keeping his mind active. Peter was the accountant of a man who has gone to prison for the murder of his wife, so Brian visits the convict hoping he will be able to assist. However, his investigation begins to encroach on the Ucos team's efforts to track down a conman who may be connected to several outstanding cases. With Rosalind Ayres and Art Malik.(n)

programme.sub-title: Coronavirus Crisis: Europe's Migrant Camps - Panorama
programme.desc: Said Reza Adib investigates conditions inside Greek migrant camps, locked down as coronavirus spread across the world, with vulnerable people havinge to share taps and toilets with those who have tested positive for the virus. Strict quarantine is enforced on camps where coronavirus cases are confirmed, leading to some shortages in food, water and medical care inside. The programme hears from charity and public health experts who warn that any failure to control the spread of the virus inside the camps could lead to potentially dangerous outbreaks.(n)

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The amateur sewer competition hosted by Joe Lycett continues. This time, the eight remaining contestants take part in a series of challenges as they return to the sewing room for lingerie and sleepwear week. To test the sewers' handling skills, judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young kick off the pattern challenge with a boned basque. The task requires sewing together small pattern pieces for a precise fit and creating a tricky hook and eye fastening. Next, in the transformation challenge, the sewers transform pyjamas and nightwear into a summer dress, before finally being tasked to create a two-piece female sleep set which has to incorporates lace somewhere in their design.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Chizzy Akudolu, Charlie Higson, Kate Williams and Tom Allen go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games across the week.(n)

programme.title: Animal Park Heroes
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Kate Humble celebrate the stars of Longleat, beginning by meeting the dedicated keepers who have been rehabilitating Asian elephant Annie - a former circus animal that was abused and mistreated.(n)

programme.title: Dig WW2 with Dan Snow
programme.desc: The historian explores Northern Ireland's role in the Second World War, revealing the story of a pilot who became the most successful U-boat hunter of all time. The programme also examines Harland & Wolff's contributions to the development of the famous Churchill tank, and takes an underwater look at the wreck of the U-155. In Donegal, a major excavation reveals the 70-year-old Spitfire flown by American pilot Bud Wolfe, who was forced to bail out just minutes after taking off.(n)

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.sub-title: The Best of RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: Monty Don and Joe Swift take a look back over the last decade of world-class design and horticulture from the RHS Chelsea Flower Show from their own gardens, beginning by celebrating the very best of British. From roses to royalty, they reveal more about the people and plants that fuel the nation's obsession with gardening, and meet the designers and the passionate people inspired by the UK's rich history and landscape.(n)

programme.title: Monkman & Seagull's Genius Adventures
programme.desc: Eric Monkman and Bobby Seagull travel around Britain as they explore their favourite scientific breakthroughs, beginning with the 18th-century inventions that kick-started the Industrial Revolution and transformed scientific understanding. They head to Derbyshire to investigate Richard Arkwright's water frame - a spinning machine that allowed cotton textiles to be mass-produced, and then examine James Watt's separate condenser, an unassuming invention that would be the biggest single improvement ever made to the steam engine.(n)

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Bill Bailey, Colin Lane and Desiree Burch join regular panellist Alan Davies to answer host Sandi Toksvig's questions on the theme 'Overseas', in an extended edition of the unorthodox quiz. During the course of the show, the panellists find out about Allotment Annies as well as who keeps their brothers in a cage. The fiendishly difficult questions lead to points being awarded for the answers the questionmaster considers most interesting, though not necessarily correct.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Being Gail Porter
programme.desc: For the first time, Gail Porter opens up about her past, hoping to understand her rise to celebrity and her fall into depression, anorexia, self-harming and homelessness.(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Three farm managers, all critical to the running of their businesses, attend an agricultural awards ceremony in London, where they battle it out for Farm Manager of the Year.(n)

programme.title: Mediterranean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The final leg of the journey takes in Morocco, where Simon visits a land borders between Africa and the European Union, and the coast of Spain, taking in the scale of the country's fruit and vegetable industry. He discovers why tourists are actively discouraged from visiting Corsica, and goes diving in waters that have the highest fish population in the Mediterranean.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Ross Kemp and Britain's Volunteer Army
programme.desc: The actor celebrates the efforts of some of the UK's 750,000 volunteers during the coronavirus crisis,, and also lends a hand in his own neighbourhood.(n)

programme.title: Your Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman and the team give horticultural tips and advice to viewers, hoping to give them the inspiration to create their own summer gardens, beginning by showing how to make the most of colour.(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: More family heirlooms are restored, including an antique skeleton clock, a giant teddy bear and a transistor radio with huge sentimental value to its owner.(n)

programme.title: The Great Interior Design Challenge
programme.desc: It's the semi-final, where two designers compete for a place in the finale. In an added twist, one of the returning contestants has been given a second chance, a 'wildcard' entry into the semi-finals, having been eliminated from the previous rounds. The competitors have 48 hours to transform a couple of rooms in modern Bristol town houses before Kelly Hoppen, Daniel Hopwood and guest judge Michelle Ogundehin decide who goes through to the next stage. Tom Dyckhoff presents.(n)

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Game show hosted by Mel Giedroyc, as pairs of contestants go head-to-head to crack a series of passwords, in an attempt to take home £2,500 in cash.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Big Dreams Small Spaces
programme.desc: Monty Don returns to help more amateur horticulturists transform their small or average-sized plots into something extraordinary. In Salisbury, newly engaged couple Glenn and Zoe want a new garden to produce flowers for their wedding, but their sloping plot seems completely unusable. Monty has the ideas and plenty of enthusiasm, but can they get the planting done in time for the wedding? In Barnet, he gets to work on an overgrown allotment that owner Nighat wants to use as a site for keeping bees, as well for growing fruit and vegetables.(n)

programme.title: The Sky's the Limit (1943)
programme.desc: A Second World War hero returns home to New York and sneaks into town incognito to avoid a big fuss. He meets the girl of his dreams, but his efforts to court her are thwarted when she leaps to the conclusion that he is a coward trying to get out of going to war. Romantic musical comedy, starring Fred Astaire and Joan Leslie, with an appearance by bandleader Freddie Slack.(n)

programme.title: My Life on a Plate
programme.desc: Brian Turner invites Tony Blackburn to take him back to the home his father designed in Poole, Dorset. The pair also visit the nearby beach where Tony used to sell ice-cream in the school holidays, and Bournemouth Pavilion where he learned the ropes as a performer and entertainer. Brian cooks a nostalgic dessert to remind Tony of his mother's cooking, and creates a culinary tribute - which puts the veteran disc jockey's life on a plate.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents highlights from some of the historic venues up and down the country and goes behind the scenes of Bamburgh Castle in Northumberland.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Kate Williams, Tom Allen, Chizzy Akudolu and Charlie Higson continue testing their general knowledge skills in assorted trivia-based games before Friday's final.(n)

programme.title: Animal Park Heroes
programme.desc: Jean Johansson gives an anteater a wash, while Ben Fogle and Kate Humble celebrate the life of Nico, Longleat Safari Park's legendary silverback gorilla.(n)

programme.title: Dig WW2 with Dan Snow
programme.desc: The historian heads out to sea to hear the story of the Empire Heritage, a cargo ship filled with Sherman tanks sunk off Malin Head during the Battle of the Atlantic in 1944. He gets the opportunity to fly a Second World War aircraft while visiting an RAF flying boat base on Lough Erne, and meets the experts intent on discovering the identities of the remains of soldiers killed during the conflict.(n)

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.sub-title: The Best of RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: Monty Don and Joe Swift embark on a horticultural trip around the globe at the world-famous flower show, discovering the people and plants that make it an international event. They are joined by Carol Klein, Adam Frost and the team to meet the international fleet of designers and plantspeople who travel thousands of miles to bring fresh ideas and fabulous floral creations to the show each year.(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.sub-title: Coronavirus: A Horizon Special - Part Two
programme.desc: Dr Chris van Tulleken, Dr Hannah Fry and Michael Mosley examine the latest research and explore some of the big questions about Covid-19 and the pandemic it has created. Michael visits the UK government's high-security laboratory Porton Down, where vaccines are being tested. He also uncovers what the experience of the 2002 SARs epidemic reveals about this one. Both were caused by coronaviruses, but certain key changes to today's virus have allowed it to infect the world, where SARs was contained.(n)

programme.title: I'll Get This - Extra Helping
programme.desc: Rob Beckett, Joanna Scanlan, Martin Kemp, Harry Redknapp and Victoria Coren Mitchell go for dinner together. They must place their bank card in the centre of the restaurant table. As the food is served and the drinks flow, they play a series of fun and revealing games, and if they win, they can retrieve their card. The person whose card remains at the end of the evening must pick up the bill for the whole group.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: David Harewood: Psychosis and Me
programme.desc: At the age of 23, David Harewood had a psychotic breakdown and was sectioned. Two years ago he opened up on Twitter about the experience - and now he wants to tell the full story. Travelling back to his home town of Birmingham, the actor lets viewers into the realities of experiencing a psychotic breakdown, and how much he blocked out at the time, meeting up with his mother and old friends who were with him at the time. But this isn't just his story. He also spends time with other young people living with psychosis and the NHS professionals who treat them.(n)

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The contestants are asked to insert an invisible zip and pockets on a pair of palazzo pants, while the judges keep a keen eye on how well they pattern-match the bold prints. For the transformation challenge, the sewers must turn towels into stylish beach cover-ups in just 90 minutes. Male models arrive in the Sewing Room for the final challenge, ready to be fitted with a made-to-measure holiday shirt using bright and colourful fabrics. Whose shirt will bring on the sunshine, and who will be sent packing?(n)

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: The team makes creative gardening use of the contents of our bins and answers viewers' gardening questions via a virtual Q&A.(n)

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: Part one of two. The documentary returns to follow the day-to-day realities the NHS faces in tackling the coronavirus pandemic, following staff and patients at London's Royal Free Trust from the first day of the lockdown. The Royal Free Hospital's emergency department is inundated with patients displaying symptoms, among them 88-year-old Peter, who is struggling for breath. He is quickly put on oxygen and moved to what was the coronary care unit, but has now become a ward for covid-positive patients.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Ross Kemp and Britain's Volunteer Army
programme.desc: The actor celebrates the heroic efforts of some of the UK's 750,000 volunteers during the coronavirus crisis.(n)

programme.title: Your Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman, Harry and David Rich, Rachel de Thame, Toby Buckland and James Wong help viewers make the most of the space in their garden - whatever its size.(n)

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Laura Jane Clark and Robert Jamison use virtual reality to redesign a 1930s Surrey house with a poorly designed layout to make better use of the space, and allow the owners to both start a family and have their parents move in with them in the future. Laura's plan involves turning the property into a light-filled interconnected series of spaces, while Robert turns conventional wisdom on its head with a visually striking and flexible design. After experiencing both potential houses, the owners must choose which they would like to put into practice.(n)

programme.title: The Great Interior Design Challenge
programme.desc: In the penultimate show, the pressure mounts as the two designers go head to head for a place in the final. They are given the challenge to transform two rooms, a living room and a dining room, in a traditional thatched cottage in Ashby St Ledgers, Northamptonshire, with only 48 hours to complete the task and a budget of £2,000 for both rooms.(n)

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Game show hosted by Mel Giedroyc, as pairs of contestants go head-to-head to crack a series of passwords, in an attempt to take home £2,500 in cash. As the teams solve the passwords, they win letters that will help them later in the game, giving them a better chance of cracking the final password which unlocks the Letterbox holding the cash prize. However, if they end up as the losing pair along the way, they'll have to surrender all the letters they've won to the winning pair - who will then go through to the semi-final eliminator round.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Big Dreams Small Spaces
programme.desc: Monty Don helps young couple Jack and Chris in Clapham to turn their tiny urban back space into a Victorian-inspired plant collector's paradise - with a twist. However, their timing is not perfect, as they are aiming to add a special dahlia collection, a fern wall and lots of modern gardening gadgets at the same time as refurbishing their home. Meanwhile, in Hereford, daughter Frankie is trying to renovate her cottage garden in memory of her sister, with the help of her mother Pat, but her ambitious plans clash with Monty's more practical approach.(n)

programme.title: Vivacious Lady (1938)
programme.desc: A professor falls in love with a brash nightclub singer while on a trip to New York, and impulsively marries her. However, he is afraid his conservative family will not approve of his new wife, so tries to keep the relationship a secret from them. Romantic comedy, with Ginger Rogers and James Stewart.(n)

programme.title: My Life on a Plate
programme.desc: Brian Turner accompanies Johnny Ball on a trip to his childhood home of Bristol, where he recalls his love of maths challenges in the classroom and the impact of ration books on the supply of sweets at his local corner shop.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: A collection of previously unseen finds from the show's travels round the country, visiting St Albans Cathedral, Wolverhampton Art Gallery, and Packwood House in Warwickshire.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Chizzy Akudolu, Charlie Higson, Kate Williams and Tom Allen go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games across the week.(n)

programme.title: Animal Park Heroes
programme.desc: Ben Fogle tries to mend his relationship with Rio the Cockatoo. Kate Humble discovers more about a giraffe that arrived in the 1960s, and cameras reveal feeding time in a new light.(n)

programme.title: Dig WW2 with Dan Snow
programme.desc: The historian travels to Lough Foyle to explore the history of a Flying Fortress that crashed while travelling from America to an air force base near Bedford. He unearths the stories of the crew's survival against the odds and learns how a local woman and her boat contributed to their rescue. He also visits the disused Cluntoe airfield in Co Tyrone, where RAF pilots were trained for flying missions all over Europe, and looks back at the climatic end to the Battle of the Atlantic on the River Foyle docks.(n)

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.sub-title: The Best of RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: Monty Don and Joe Swift reveal the trendsetters amongst the designers and growers at the RHS Chelsea Flower Show, and discover who has influenced our gardening habits. Elsewhere, fashion designer Paul Smith shares what inspires him at the show year after year.(n)

programme.title: Vienna Blood
programme.desc: Oskar is called away from Max and Clara's engagement party to investigate the brutal murders of three women in a brothel. Clara understands Max's eagerness to join the investigation and allows him to follow, much to the detective's dismay. The police almost immediately arrest Victor Krull, a man who regularly visited the brothel and took a particular interest in one of the victims, whose body has been washed and ritualistically displayed. The press are informed of the arrest, but Max is not at all convinced.(n)

programme.title: Comedy Shorts
programme.desc: A filmic offshoot of Tim Key's stage show Megadate, in which a man dazzles a woman on the perfect first date. However, she doesn't text him back, leading him to retrace his steps. His path is riddled with cynics and naysayers, questioning his optimistic outlook on how the date went. The nerd in the bowling alley who thought he'd embarrassed himself, the old eel catcher on the mud flats trying to douse his dreams with wisdom, and the compassionate waitress at the chicken shop. Starring Tim Key, Mandeep Dhillon, Sam Fletcher, Bill Paterson and Julie Dray.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Alastair Campbell: Depression and Me
programme.desc: The journalist and former Downing Street press secretary - best known as Tony Blair's 'spin doctor' during his tenure as prime minister - talks about his lifelong battle with depression. Encouraged by his family, Alastair sets out on a journey to explore if cutting-edge science can offer him, and the millions of people like him, the hope of one day living free of the illness. As he tries to better understand his depression, he also reflects on key events in his life and asks if they could have had a negative effect on his mind.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene that shows even a tiny shanty by a pond can be a warm place in the dead of winter.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of Skin
programme.desc: Biologist Ben Garrod examines the evolution of skin - the largest organ in any creature's body - across all species of the animal kingdom, beginning by looking at how that of vertebrates has allowed them to conquer the planet.(n)

programme.title: Andrew Marr's History of Modern Britain
programme.desc: The journalist considers whether the often-accepted view that Britain entered a golden age in the 1950s glosses over the social and racial tensions of the decade. He also examines how the new breed of 1960s satirists taught the public to laugh at their rulers and how factions including the Campaign for Nuclear Disarmament began to protest against those in power.(n)

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Tom Baker narrates a programme examining the evolution of the loaf in Britain and how the bread that people consumed in the past had a lot to say about their social status. From the time when white bread was exclusively for the rich, to the moment when dietary experts recommended brown loaves for better health, this documentary explains how habits in buying and eating bread have changed.(n)

programme.title: Arena
programme.sub-title: The Changin' Times of Ike White - Arena
programme.desc: Documentary in which Dan Vernon tries to track down Ike White, a musical prodigy who was serving life in prison for murder when he was given the opportunity to record an album. The resulting record Changin' Times came out in 1976 and following his release from prison, Whiteseemed to be on the path to redemption, but just as he was charting a course to stardom, he disappeared.(n)

programme.title: Morocco to Timbuktu: An Arabian Adventure
programme.desc: Writer and explorer Alice Morrison embarks on a camel trek over the dunes of the Sahara, 3.5 million square miles of desert, for the second and final part of her journey to Timbuktu. Along the way she visits a livestock market that centuries ago provided merchants with their camels, before reaching her final destination. Far from the legendary City of Gold, Timbuktu is a city where UN solders keep a fragile peace after an incursion by Islamic extremists in 2012. Alice relives the city's glorious past with a visit to its world famous mosques, meets the Tuaregs - desert nomads who have sought refuge in there - and celebrates at a party with her newfound friends.(n)

programme.title: Elegance and Decadence: The Age of the Regency
programme.sub-title: The Many and the Few - A Divided Decade
programme.desc: Lucy Worsley reviews the final years of George's time as Prince Regent, which culminated in his coronation as King George IV in 1821. Writers including Lord Byron and Percy and Mary Shelley railed against his excesses, while industrial technologies transformed the British landscape - but rapidly expanding cities like Birmingham, Liverpool and Manchester remained unrepresented in Parliament due to the country's archaic form of government.(n)

programme.title: Vienna: Empire, Dynasty and Dream
programme.desc: Simon Sebag Montefiore follows the Habsburgs to their dramatic demise. From his struggles with Napoleon III and Bismarck and the suicide of his son Rudolf, to the assassination of his beautiful wife Sisi, Emperor Franz Josef's empire and his family proved impossible to control - but while the Habsburgs headed for extinction, Vienna blossomed.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene that shows even a tiny shanty by a pond can be a warm place in the dead of winter.(n)

programme.title: Andrew Marr's History of Modern Britain
programme.desc: The journalist considers whether the often-accepted view that Britain entered a golden age in the 1950s glosses over the social and racial tensions of the decade. He also examines how the new breed of 1960s satirists taught the public to laugh at their rulers and how factions including the Campaign for Nuclear Disarmament began to protest against those in power.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints an old dirt road leading up to a quaint country barn nestled in nature's greenery.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of Skin
programme.desc: Ben Garrod explores how skin has evolved to enable animals to move in a range of habitats, revealing how adaptations allow birds to fly, sharks to speed through water and sugar gliders to take to the air.(n)

programme.title: Chemistry - A Volatile History
programme.desc: Professor of Theoretical Physics Jim Al-Khalili follows in the footsteps of the pioneers who discovered and mapped the elements, examining why the belief that there were only four of them - earth, fire, air and water - lasted until the 19th century. He also investigates the rivalries that dogged scientific progress and explores how new approaches to splitting matter created the science of chemistry.(n)

programme.title: The First Georgians: The German Kings Who Made Britain
programme.desc: Dr Lucy Worsley reveals the history of Britain under the reigns of George I and George II, in a programme originally shown to mark the 300th anniversary of the Hanoverian succession to the British throne. She is given access to treasure from the Royal Collection at the Queen's Gallery, Buckingham Palace, to give a personal insight into the monarchs' lives, and begins by charting the story of George I's feuding family.(n)

programme.title: Television's Opening Night: How the Box Was Born
programme.desc: Dallas Campbell, Professor Danielle George and Dr Hugh Hunt mark the 80th anniversary of Britain's very first TV broadcast by trying to recreate the landmark event. The inaugural programme came live from Alexandria Palace on November 2, 1936, but there are no surviving recordings of the show. In response, the presenter trio set out to recreate every aspect of this first broadcast, from the variety acts featured right down to the camera equipment used, using authentic technology and filming techniques. Plus, pioneering TV engineer John Logie Baird's assistant - now aged 104 - is on hand to lend some technical assistance, and Dallas meets a one of the performers who appeared on the first programme to find out what it is like to be part of TV history.(n)

programme.title: Tomorrow's Worlds: The Unearthly History of Science Fiction
programme.desc: Dominic Sandbrook explores the many aspects of the genre and examines its impact on cinema, television and literature. He begins by looking at sci-fi's enduring fascination with outer space, from Jules Verne's pioneering 19th-century vision of a voyage to the moon, to the universe George Lucas created for Star Wars. Along the way, he reveals how Stanley Kubrick made 2001: A Space Odyssey seem so believable and why a man in a dressing gown became one of sci-fi's best-loved heroes in The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy. Featuring contributions by Star Trek's William Shatner and Nichelle Nichols, Anthony Daniels and Kenny Baker from the Star Wars movies, Avatar's Zoe Saldana, Dark Star director John Carpenter and author Neil Gaiman.(n)

programme.title: Arena
programme.sub-title: The Changin' Times of Ike White - Arena
programme.desc: Documentary in which Daniel Vernon tries to track down Ike White, a musical prodigy who was serving life in prison for murder when he was given the opportunity to record an album. The resulting record Changin' Times came out in 1976 and following his release from prison, Whiteseemed to be on the path to redemption, but just as he was charting a course to stardom, he disappeared.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints an old dirt road leading up to a quaint country barn nestled in nature's greenery.(n)

programme.title: The First Georgians: The German Kings Who Made Britain
programme.desc: Dr Lucy Worsley reveals the history of Britain under the reigns of George I and George II, in a programme originally shown to mark the 300th anniversary of the Hanoverian succession to the British throne. She is given access to treasure from the Royal Collection at the Queen's Gallery, Buckingham Palace, to give a personal insight into the monarchs' lives, and begins by charting the story of George I's feuding family.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a beautiful monochromatic scene with delicate leafless trees and a rich grassy meadow cuddling a sweet cottage.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of Skin
programme.desc: Ben Garrod explores how skin acts as a barrier between animals and the outside world, shielding them from extremes of temperature, the harmful effects of UV and a host of organisms.(n)

programme.title: Michael Wood's Story of England
programme.sub-title: Henry VIII to the Industrial Revolution
programme.desc: The broadcaster tracks Kibworth's 17th-century dissenters, explores the Grand Union Canal, a waterway that connects Birmingham with London, and discovers the achievements of a pioneering 18th-century feminist writer. The descendants of a highwayman transported to Australia uncover their roots, and the effects of the Industrial Revolution on the village are examined.(n)

programme.title: Climbing Blind
programme.desc: Documentary about Jesse Dufton and his attempt to take on the challenge of becoming the first blind person to lead a climb of the Old Man of Hoy in Orkney, Scotland. At four years of age, Jesse was diagnosed with retinitis pigmentosa, a rare genetic disease that breaks down the retina's cells. When he was 20, Jesse could no longer read. By the time he was 30, he could only detect light, with around a one to two per cent field of view. As a lifelong climber, what Jesse has achieved flies in the face of adversity, training for world cup events and leading traditional rock climbs with his sight guide and fiancee Molly. Now, his attempt on the Old Man of Hoy is testimony to his ambition to take on new and greater challenges, despite his devastating condition.(n)

programme.title: Invasion! With Sam Willis
programme.desc: Sam Willis tells the story of the Barbary Corsaire pirates who made their headquarters on Lundy Island. He also sheds light on the little-known tale of King Louis the Lion, a French King who invaded Britain in the 13th century.(n)

programme.title: The Quest for Bannockburn
programme.desc: Part two of two. Neil Oliver and Tony Pollard conclude their attempt to mark the 700th anniversary of Bannockburn by pinpointing the location of Scotland's most iconic battlefield. They are joined by historians, archaeologists and environmental scientists as they gather evidence to chart the events that ended in Robert the Bruce's overwhelming victory over the English that helped seal Scotland's future as an independent kingdom.(n)

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Sue Ellen is excited to meet her pen pal in person, but is disappointed when she realises they do not have as much in common as she thought.(n)

programme.title: Dennis & Gnasher Unleashed!
programme.desc: The gang spots what appears to be a meteor through Screwtop's telescope.(n)

programme.title: Junior Bake Off

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: Coronavirus Crisis: Europe's Migrant Camps - Panorama
programme.desc: Said Reza Adib investigates conditions inside Greek migrant camps, locked down as coronavirus spread across the world, with vulnerable people havinge to share taps and toilets with those who have tested positive for the virus. Strict quarantine is enforced on camps where coronavirus cases are confirmed, leading to some shortages in food, water and medical care inside. The programme hears from charity and public health experts who warn that any failure to control the spread of the virus inside the camps could lead to potentially dangerous outbreaks.(n)

programme.title: Life and Birth
programme.desc: First-time mum Mini is induced three weeks early after experiencing reduced fetal movement, and with concerns growing it is ultimately decided to deliver the baby by caesarean section. Meanwhile, 19-year-old Billie-Joe has a rare twin pregnancy called TRAP Sequence, which means one of the twins hasn't developed properly and has no heartbeat. Supported by her boyfriend Josh, Billie-Joe undergoes a complex procedure at Birmingham Women's Hospital's Fetal Medicine Centre to save the life of their healthy baby.(n)

programme.title: The A Word
programme.desc: Eddie surprises Maurice with some good news, revealing that Scott's Brewery is such a success that a company in London are interested in purchasing it for more than £1million. In Manchester, Ben asks Alison if she and Joe would like to go out for the day, and Joe later joins Paul for a camping trip in the Lakes, organised by Bill's mum Sarah. Paul invites Eddie along for one last hurrah, but tensions quickly flare when Eddie discovers that Maurice has been invited too. Father and son end up bickering around the campfire as the rain sets in.(n)

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Documentary following two men preparing to compete in the World Stone Skimming Championships, held on the tiny west coast island of Easdale in Scotland. Dougie is eight-time World Stone Skimming champion and native Scot from Blairgowrie, and although he is confident, newcomer Alex threatens his legendary status. The programme charts the determination and dedication of the competitors, and reveals what it truly takes to come out on top in this unique contest.(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the restorers get to work on more family heirlooms, including a battered, weather-beaten dictionary believed to have been the personal possession of Lord Horatio Nelson and used on board his flagship, HMS Victory. The other items are a quirky train set built by the man behind the inventions in Chitty Chitty Bang Bang, a sailor's trinket box and a doll from 1947.(n)

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The amateur sewer competition hosted by Joe Lycett continues. This time, the eight remaining contestants take part in a series of challenges as they return to the sewing room for lingerie and sleepwear week. To test the sewers' handling skills, judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young kick off the pattern challenge with a boned basque. The task requires sewing together small pattern pieces for a precise fit and creating a tricky hook and eye fastening. Next, in the transformation challenge, the sewers transform pyjamas and nightwear into a summer dress, before finally being tasked to create a two-piece female sleep set which has to incorporates lace somewhere in their design.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: Coronavirus Crisis: Europe's Migrant Camps - Panorama
programme.desc: Said Reza Adib investigates conditions inside Greek migrant camps, locked down as coronavirus spread across the world, with vulnerable people havinge to share taps and toilets with those who have tested positive for the virus. Strict quarantine is enforced on camps where coronavirus cases are confirmed, leading to some shortages in food, water and medical care inside. The programme hears from charity and public health experts who warn that any failure to control the spread of the virus inside the camps could lead to potentially dangerous outbreaks.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tina calls an ambulance for Jean and Suki steps in to help - but when she realises all is not as it seems, she tries to manipulate the situation to her favour. Callum and Kathy worry that Ben is unwell, putting his surgery in doubt, while Linda is taken aback when Phil seems more concerned about buying the Vic than his son's operation. Keegan's actions leave Tiffany torn, Isaac opens up to Denise and Ian plays Habiba at her own game.(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedians Greg Davies and Guz Khan, Strictly co-host Claudia Winkleman and historian Lucy Worsley join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies. Savvy viewers may have noted that after 12 series, both Mitchell and Mack are running woefully short of truthful bizarre experiences to recount of themselves.(n)

programme.title: Normal People
programme.desc: Connell bumps into Marianne back in Sligo and despite the awkwardness between them he offers to attend her father's anniversary mass the next day. Back in Dublin, the two study hard for the Trinity Scholarship exams, but Connell is mugged on a night out after the results are announced and he turns to Marianne for help. On arriving at her house, he sees that he has interrupted a get-together, which includes Jamie. Adaptation of Sally Rooney's novel, starring Paul Mescal and Daisy Edgar-Jones.(n)

programme.title: Normal People
programme.desc: It is the summer holidays and Connell arrives at Marianne's family house in Italy, but the obvious chemistry between him and Marianne causes friction with Jamie. programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: Coronavirus Crisis: Europe's Migrant Camps - Panorama
programme.desc: Said Reza Adib investigates conditions inside Greek migrant camps, locked down as coronavirus spread across the world, with vulnerable people havinge to share taps and toilets with those who have tested positive for the virus. Strict quarantine is enforced on camps where coronavirus cases are confirmed, leading to some shortages in food, water and medical care inside. The programme hears from charity and public health experts who warn that any failure to control the spread of the virus inside the camps could lead to potentially dangerous outbreaks.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tina calls an ambulance for Jean and Suki steps in to help - but when she realises all is not as it seems, she tries to manipulate the situation to her favour. Callum and Kathy worry that Ben is unwell, putting his surgery in doubt, while Linda is taken aback when Phil seems more concerned about buying the Vic than his son's operation. Keegan's actions leave Tiffany torn, Isaac opens up to Denise and Ian plays Habiba at her own game.(n)

programme.title: Home Ground
programme.desc: in this lambing special, Gavin Andrews and Jo Scott spend the day helping out at Crosby Cleland's farm outside Saintfield, Co Down. Plus, Jo meets a nine-year-old boy who is running his own poultry business.(n)

programme.title: Normal People
programme.desc: Connell bumps into Marianne back in Sligo and despite the awkwardness between them he offers to attend her father's anniversary mass the next day. Back in Dublin, the two study hard for the Trinity Scholarship exams, but Connell is mugged on a night out after the results are announced and he turns to Marianne for help. On arriving at her house, he sees that he has interrupted a get-together, which includes Jamie. Adaptation of Sally Rooney's novel, starring Paul Mescal and Daisy Edgar-Jones.(n)

programme.title: Normal People
programme.desc: It is the summer holidays and Connell arrives at Marianne's family house in Italy, but the obvious chemistry between him and Marianne causes friction with Jamie. Peggy cooks a lavish meal, but tensions run high during dinner, with Jamie drinking too much and picking a fight with Marianne.(n)

Ruby is impressed by the way Martin tackles a thief and offers him a job at the club.(n)

programme.title: Life and Birth
programme.desc: First-time mum Mini is induced three weeks early after experiencing reduced fetal movement, and with concerns growing it is ultimately decided to deliver the baby by caesarean section. Meanwhile, 19-year-old Billie-Joe has a rare twin pregnancy called TRAP Sequence, which means one of the twins hasn't developed properly and has no heartbeat. Supported by her boyfriend Josh, Billie-Joe undergoes a complex procedure at Birmingham Women's Hospital's Fetal Medicine Centre to save the life of their healthy baby.(n)

programme.title: The A Word
programme.desc: Eddie surprises Maurice with some good news, revealing that Scott's Brewery is such a success that a company in London are interested in purchasing it for more than £1million. In Manchester, Ben asks Alison if she and Joe would like to go out for the day, and Joe later joins Paul for a camping trip in the Lakes, organised by Bill's mum Sarah. Paul invites Eddie along for one last hurrah, but tensions quickly flare when Eddie discovers that Maurice has been invited too. Father and son end up bickering around the campfire as the rain sets in.(n)

programme.title: My Mate's a Muslim
programme.desc: As Muslims, rapper Krept and vlogger Rumena both look forward to the holy month of Ramadan as a time for reflection and self-improvement, but this year will be a little different. In the year of the pandemic, there will be no mosques with fellow worshippers, no late-night shisha with friends and no full-on feasts with the family. Here, Krept and Rumena challenge their non-Muslim friends Kritikal and Olivia to join them in a day of no eating and drinking to learn about the true experience of Ramadan.(n)

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Documentary following two men preparing to compete in the World Stone Skimming Championships, held on the tiny west coast island of Easdale in Scotland. Dougie is eight-time World Stone Skimming champion and native Scot from Blairgowrie, and although he is confident, newcomer Alex threatens his legendary status. The programme charts the determination and dedication of the competitors, and reveals what it truly takes to come out on top in this unique contest.(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the restorers get to work on more family heirlooms, including a battered, weather-beaten dictionary believed to have been the personal possession of Lord Horatio Nelson and used on board his flagship, HMS Victory. The other items are a quirky train set built by the man behind the inventions in Chitty Chitty Bang Bang, a sailor's trinket box and a doll from 1947.(n)

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The amateur sewer competition hosted by Joe Lycett continues. This time, the eight remaining contestants take part in a series of challenges as they return to the sewing room for lingerie and sleepwear week. To test the sewers' handling skills, judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young kick off the pattern challenge with a boned basque. The task requires sewing together small pattern pieces for a precise fit and creating a tricky hook and eye fastening. Next, in the transformation challenge, the sewers transform pyjamas and nightwear into a summer dress, before finally being tasked to create a two-piece female sleep set which has to incorporates lace somewhere in their design.(n)

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: While some countries are claiming success in their effort to curb Covid-19, Brazil is increasingly looking like an outlier. The rate of new infections is still increasing, the death toll is mounting, and all the while President Jair Bolsonaro focuses on easing social distancing and reopening the Brazilian economy. Stephen Sackur speaks to Brazil's former Health Minister Luiz Henrique Mandetta who was fired by Mr Bolsonaro after a series of disagreements. Is President Bolsonaro putting his nation at risk?(n)

programme.title: Select Committees

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: Coronavirus Crisis: Europe's Migrant Camps - Panorama
programme.desc: Said Reza Adib investigates conditions inside Greek migrant camps, locked down as coronavirus spread across the world, with vulnerable people havinge to share taps and toilets with those who have tested positive for the virus. Strict quarantine is enforced on camps where coronavirus cases are confirmed, leading to some shortages in food, water and medical care inside. The programme hears from charity and public health experts who warn that any failure to control the spread of the virus inside the camps could lead to potentially dangerous outbreaks.(n)

Alan Titchmarsh's challenge is to create a more child-friendly garden for their terraced home in Tonbridge, and his first task is to make a shady environment so the kids can spend time outside in the day. After that, he and the team come up with a bespoke lighting system for the evenings, acoustic fencing to cut down the noise from a nearby railway line, and an area for the parents.(n)

programme.title: Harry's Heroes: Euro Having a Laugh
programme.desc: The second leg of the tour takes the boys to Italy, where they're holing up at a traditional Palazzo in Florence. Assistant Manager John Barnes thinks the lads need a bit of culture, so sends them off sightseeing to take in the Ponte Vecchio and Michelangelo's David. At training, fitness coach Luke Worthington puts the boys through their paces, but it's not looking good as the team is carrying so many injuries there are only three fit players. Plus, they've lost one of their biggest characters - Neil 'Razor' Ruddock - who had to leave the tour in Paris to return to London for tests on his heart.(n)

Later, they travel to a place renowned for its spiritual enlightenment, but a therapeutic conversation takes a decidedly different turn as the brothers finally get to have their say and talk to Dougie about how they felt when he left the family home when they were children.(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: The comedian follows the work of Battersea Dogs & Cats Home in London, meeting staff involved in treating, training and rehoming animals. This time, Paul meets a 10-month-old puppy who has been brought into Battersea twice for being too boisterous, before helping to soothe a pointer cross who has had a hard life.(n)

programme.title: Harry's Heroes: Euro Having a Laugh
programme.desc: In the final episode, Harry, John and the team embark on the final leg of the tour in Germany, as they prepare to face their arch-rivals at the Wacker Arena in Burghausen. After checking into their Hotel in the Bavarian Alps, Harry receives the German team sheet, leaving him concerned his team are going to have to massively step up their game. With the fear of the match going to penalties hanging over their head, Harry brings in a German psychologist to offer some advice. Finally the big day arrives, and Harry has managed to recruit Manchester United legend Teddy Sheringham, so the team finally has its missing striker.(n)

programme.title: Harry's Heroes: Euro Having a Laugh
programme.desc: The second leg of the tour takes the boys to Italy, where they're holing up at a traditional Palazzo in Florence. At training, fitness coach Luke Worthington puts the boys through their paces, but it's not looking good as the team is carrying so many injuries there are only three fit players. Plus, they've lost one of their biggest characters - Neil 'Razor' Ruddock - who had to leave the tour in Paris to return to London for tests on his heart.(n)

programme.desc: Harry Hill introduces another rib-tickling collection of camcorder calamities and home-video howlers, featuring practical jokers, naughty animals, musical mishaps and holiday clips.(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a selection of comical clips filmed by viewers on their camcorders and mobile phones, featuring a Prince Harry impersonator, boys being boys, and a DJ.(n)

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: Jeff leaves Cloud 9 to work at a rival shop, while Garrett convinces Glenn that he is poaching employees. Comedy, starring America Ferrera and Colton Dunn.(n)

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: The employees worry when their new district manager arrives. Elsewhere, Amy and Jonah search for ways to raise money so that no one is fired.(n)

programme.title: Dinner Date
programme.desc: Jake from London selects three blind dates from five potential partners, based on the menus they have created. After dining with the girls, he chooses to see one of them again.(n)

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Guys from Manchester, Liverpool and Doncaster compete for the affections of property developer Chelsea.(n)

programme.title: Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show, asking actor Tim Healy, Towie star-turned-presenter Mark Wright and Coronation Street's Alexandra Mardell (Emma Brooker) to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. One of them will make it through to the Super Catchphrase round, where they could win £50,000 for the charity of their choice.(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Comedian Harry Hill narrates a selection of camcorder calamities and viewers' mobile-phone footage, featuring clever animals, out-of-control teenagers and painful encounters with basketballs.(n)

programme.title: The Ellen DeGeneres Show
programme.desc: The host talks to Kristen Bell, who tells her all about her Ellen digital series, Momsplaining with Kristen Bell.(n)

programme.title: Dinner Date
programme.desc: Hannah from Berkshire selects three blind dates from five potential partners, based on the menus they have created. After dining with them, she chooses to see one of them again.(n)

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: In Manchester, Max, Denison and Callum go up against each other in a series of shopping challenges hoping to win the affections of Newcastle model Sophiah.(n)

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: The superstore plays host to local small businesses, including Glenn's wife who has come to sell her hand-made needlepoint - leading to accusations of favouritism.(n)

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: Dina motivates the employees to sell as many tickets as possible as lottery fever hits the store. Jonah helps Amy track down the new district manager so she can ask for a raise. Comedy, starring America Ferrera.(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Surreal comedy from the mind of Harry Hill, inspired by viewers' home videos, featuring everything from giddy grandmas, cute kids and crazy animals to wedding nightmares, below-the-belt mishaps, practical jokers and painful-looking accidents.(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring everything from giddy grandmas, cute kids and crazy animals to below-the-belt mishaps, practical jokers and painful-looking accidents.(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring the parrot cure for America's obesity problem and Dermot O'Leary being bullied by the X Factor crew.(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring everything from giddy grandmas, cute kids and crazy animals to below-the-belt mishaps, practical jokers and painful-looking accidents.(n)

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Walden and Alan think it may be time for Berta to retire when she injures her back at work. However, searching for her replacement isn't going to be easy. Comedy, starring Jon Cryer, Ashton Kutcher and Conchata Ferrell.(n)

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan and Lyndsey's affair is under threat of exposure when their friend, Stephanie, catches them at a hotel. Lyndsey persuades Alan to ask Stephanie on a date to not raise suspicion. Meanwhile, Walden and Jenny wake up in bed together after a night partying. Comedy, starring Jon Cryer and Ashton Kutcher.(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian goes to collect Stewie from Lois's parents' house in Palm Springs - but the duo lose their plane tickets in an airport bar, and find themselves with no choice but to stow away on board a train back to Quahog. Meanwhile, the Griffins try to spice up their marriage.(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: A new drug craze hits town, prompting Peter to assume the guise of new student Lando Griffin to eradicate the problem at Meg's school, where he extols the virtues of clean living with a song-and-dance routine inspired by Grease. Animated adventure, with the voices of Seth MacFarlane and Mila Kunis.(n)

programme.title: Plebs
programme.desc: The boys are desperate for tickets to one of the 'beast shows' at the Games, while Marcus meets Delphine, a fiery activist from Gaul, and Grumio clashes with Landlady, their missing landlord's formidable mother. Comedy, starring Tom Rosenthal, Joel Fry and Ryan Sampson.(n)

programme.title: Plebs
programme.desc: Grumio meets Justin, the father who left him on a hill as a baby and who now wants to make up for lost time, and Marcus and Delphine come to terms with their respective pasts.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jack has second thoughts about buying the shop. Liz is delighted when Steve phones to say he and Vicky are married. Reg is stunned when Eric makes a big announcement.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jack decides to throw a party for his neighbours. Percy commemorates VJ Day in the Rovers. Reg attempts to convince the Duckworths to think again about buying the shop.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The police arrive at the Windsors to find Scott still missing. Rachel gives Chris an ultimatum. Nick tells Pete the whole story about Kim's threat to Alice.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank is set on revenge and Rachel reaches the end of the line with Chris.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Eileen Needham sues for divorce after a domestic dispute gets out of hand, and her hard-nosed solicitor Sylvia Swinton (played by Outnumbered actress Samantha Bond) does little to calm the situation, so Joe steps in to play the peacemaker. Stan Bickle arrives in Aidensfield, bringing his daughter Josie (guest star Jenna Boyd), whose flirtatious ways steal David's heart and bring out the worst in Peggy.(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.sub-title: Climb the Greasy Pole - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. A seat becomes vacant on the local council when Sam Popham is taken ill, so Pop stands in the election, and Mariette returns from holiday with Charley. David Jason stars.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: A housing shortage forces a compromise between the flatmates and their disapproving landlord. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth helps an old friend find refuge at country house Greenshaw's Folly, but a sinister shadow falls over the strange building's various inhabitants and a murderer strikes. Miss Marple uncovers secrets from the past about Miss Greenshaw as a dangerous storm gathers and the bodies start to pile up. Julia McKenzie heads an all-star cast that includes Jim Moir (better known as Vic Reeves), Kimberley Nixon, Fiona Shaw, Robert Glenister, Julia Sawalha, Judy Parfitt and Matt Willis (formerly of pop group Busted).(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Oscar's golfing pal asks him to investigate the disappearance of his son, who left home after a blazing row with his overbearing father and has been spotted frequenting a club in Whitby. Meanwhile, the Aidensfield police officers are baffled by the activities of a modern-day Robin Hood, who has committed a string of burglaries and given away his ill-gotten gains to the elderly residents of a new block of flats. Drama, starring Derek Fowlds.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Passions run high as Frank finally confronts Kim and Dave.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim attempts to convince Frank that the baby is his and not Dave's. Frank spends the night at Zoe's and feels like an old fool. Mandy tries to flirt with Dave in the tearoom.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Father Donnelly breaks some tragic news to Don. Curly, Kevin, Jim, Andy and Des form a five-a-side football team. Jack has some fun looking at expensive cars.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vera is shocked by Ivy's death. Curly signs his team up to a five-a-side league. Nick receives a letter from Ivy in the event of her death which contains some stunning news.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin comes home with a puppy, to Chrissy's dismay. Flat-share comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan, Sally Thomsett and Paula Wilcox.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Case of the Missing Will
programme.desc: The sleuth sets out to solve the baffling murder of a Cambridge don who met with foul play shortly after deciding to change his will. Starring David Suchet, Beth Goddard and Mark Kingston.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Oscar's golfing pal asks him to investigate the disappearance of his son, who left home after a blazing row with his overbearing father and has been spotted frequenting a club in Whitby. Meanwhile, the Aidensfield police officers are baffled by the activities of a modern-day Robin Hood, who has committed a string of burglaries and given away his ill-gotten gains to the elderly residents of a new block of flats. Drama, starring Derek Fowlds.(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: First episode of the costume drama set in an Edwardian country house in 1912, written by Oscar-winning writer Julian Fellowes. The lives of the Crawley family and Downton Abbey's servants are changed for ever when the sinking of the Titanic leaves the estate without its heir and his son. The arrival of a new valet shocks the staff, who scheme to make his stay as short-lived as possible. Starring Maggie Smith, Hugh Bonneville and Elizabeth McGovern.(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The newly promoted Detective Sergeant Morse investigates when a famous international thief tries to steal a Faberge egg from an auction. The case leads to the gruesome murders of an academic and a gangster, and Morse is convinced the deaths are the work of the same man. As he struggles with his new responsibilities, he finds himself forced to mentor a younger partner (played by Poldark's Lewis Peek).(n)

programme.title: Bancroft
programme.desc: Promoted to Detective Chief Superintendent, Bancroft has professional success but at great personal cost. When a double murder brings an unexpected reunion, Elizabeth is forced onto the sidelines by Detective Superintendent Jake Harper. However, as new enemies emerge, old foes make their move. Thriller, starring Sarah Parish, David Avery, Charlotte Hope, Adam Long and Lee Boardman.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The mysterious death of a former police commissioner forces a reluctant Morse to team up with old professional rival Chief Inspector Dawson. As they and Sergeant Lewis begin to investigate, a link is uncovered to the unsolved case of a girl murdered 18 years previously. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately, Kenneth Colley and Christopher Eccleston.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: Peril at End House - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. While holidaying in Cornwall with his friend Captain Hastings, the sleuth meets a local woman who has seemingly become unaccountably accident-prone - but soon starts to suspect she is actually a victim of foul play. David Suchet and Hugh Fraser star.(n)
