programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.sub-title: Climb the Greasy Pole - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. Pop and Ma become the target of dirty tricks and smear tactics during the election campaign, and Charley is placed in a compromising position by Abigail's teacher.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Jo try to foil a showdown at the Mucky Duck Saloon when Robin and loud Irishman Mick get into an argument. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Newlyweds Mike and Ellie decide to build their dream home at a beauty spot called Gypsy's Acre, ignoring the warnings of elusive romany Mrs Lee about an ancient curse supposedly cast upon the land. The couple settle into their new life, but the mysterious woman makes her presence felt, subjecting them to a campaign of hatred in an attempt to force them out. Tragedy soon strikes, but as luck would have it Miss Marple is staying nearby with her recently widowed old friend Mrs Phillpot. However, if the spinster sleuth is to solve the puzzle, she must put her own life in grave danger. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with Wendy Craig, Glynis Barber, Hugh Dennis, Tamzin Outhwaite, Tom Hughes, Joanna Vanderham, Aneurin Barnard, William Hope and Janet Henfrey.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A disgruntled client falls under suspicion when an insurance salesman is found murdered, but the victim's complicated private life gives Mason a lot to think about before the case is solved. Alf Ventress makes himself at home in the Aidensfield Arms while his house undergoes repairs, and soon overstays his welcome, while a drunk locked up in the cells for a night turns out to be a war hero friend of Sgt Miller fallen on hard times.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Linda tells Viv that she and Biff are no longer together. Frank makes it clear that as soon as Kim is well he wants her out. Dave sneaks into Kim's bedroom.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Ned and Roy are amused to see Zak jogging. Kim has no intention of leaving Home Farm with nothing and calls her solicitor. Jack and Sarah are fed up with looking after Joseph.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Nick agrees with Gail and Martin not to say anything about Ivy's letter. At the funeral tea, Vera snaps and tells Josie she shouldn't be in Ivy's house.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jack is horrified to learn that his inheritance makes him ineligible for claiming social. The Platts try to make Nick see that Ivy's letter isn't a will and might not be valid.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective's friend Sir Charles Cartwright (guest star Martin Shaw) hosts a cocktail party at his home in Cornwall, where an amiable local priest chokes to death on his drink. There seems to be no motive for murder, and no trace of poison found in the victim's glass, but weeks later, when Sir Charles's doctor friend Sir Bartholomew Strange dies in similar circumstances, he and Poirot team up to investigate. Drama, with Art Malik, Jane Asher and Anna Carteret, and David Suchet in the title role.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A disgruntled client falls under suspicion when an insurance salesman is found murdered, but the victim's complicated private life gives Mason a lot to think about before the case is solved. Alf Ventress makes himself at home in the Aidensfield Arms while his house undergoes repairs, and soon overstays his welcome, while a drunk locked up in the cells for a night turns out to be a war hero friend of Sgt Miller fallen on hard times.(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Everyone is anxious at the arrival of Matthew Crawley, the heir presumptive, but he soon encounters problems settling into his new life in Downton, and his mother Isobel immediately crosses swords with Violet. Meanwhile, butler Carson is mortified to be confronted with his past, and housemaid Gwen seems to have a secret correspondent. Costume drama, starring Maggie Smith, Hugh Bonneville, Dan Stevens, Penelope Wilton, Elizabeth McGovern, Jim Carter and Rose Leslie.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: John Barnaby arrives in Midsomer and the headmistress of an upmarket girls' boarding school wastes no time in asking him to patrol a forthcoming classic-car show. However, when the local DJ who was set to judge the vehicles is crushed to death, the detective is soon investigating his first murder case since replacing cousin Tom - and as the death toll rises, he finds himself in a perilous situation. Drama, guest starring David Warner and Samantha Bond, with Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes.(n)

programme.title: Bancroft
programme.desc: As the murder evidence begins to build, Harper and the team are met with conflicting accounts. Meanwhile, Elizabeth finds herself taunted by one of the suspects and the trouble around the Kamara deal, forcing her to choose between her personal and professional loyalties. As the chief superintendent is deserted by her allies, she enters a dangerous game of cat and mouse. Crime thriller, starring Sarah Parish, David Avery and Charlotte Hope.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The fatal poisoning of a deaconess who was about to become Oxford's first female chaplain makes Morse wonder whether the city's conservative clerics could be hiding a deadly secret. Further investigation uncovers a connection between the murder and a weight loss clinic. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately and Zoe Wanamaker (My Family).(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: Peril at End House - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Belgian sleuth's Cornish holiday comes to a dramatic conclusion as he unravels the mystery behind the series of disastrous near-misses that placed the life of a beautiful heiress in danger, and as the truth comes to light, a secret engagement is revealed. David Suchet stars, with Philip Jackson and Hugh Fraser.(n)

programme.desc: Chrissy and Jo try to foil a showdown at the Mucky Duck Saloon when Robin and loud Irishman Mick get into an argument. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: The season of goodwill descends on the Larkin household, and the family celebrates the 'perfick' Christmas. Drama, based on the characters created by HE Bates, starring David Jason, Pam Ferris and Catherine Zeta-Jones.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin and Jo become concerned when Chrissy starts dating an older man - so they decide to play gooseberry. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective's friend Sir Charles Cartwright (guest star Martin Shaw) hosts a cocktail party at his home in Cornwall, where an amiable local priest chokes to death on his drink. There seems to be no motive for murder, and no trace of poison found in the victim's glass, but weeks later, when Sir Charles's doctor friend Sir Bartholomew Strange dies in similar circumstances, he and Poirot team up to investigate. Drama, with Art Malik, Jane Asher and Anna Carteret, and David Suchet in the title role.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Sergeant Nokes arrives to take charge after a lorry driver is savagely beaten and his consignment of condemned meat is stolen on the way to the incinerator, causing a health alert in Aidensfield. Peggy gets involved when she is asked to house-sit Mrs Grant's mansion and takes advantage of the opportunity to run a makshift B&B, only to find herself under arrest for handling stolen goods.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sarah panics when Chris takes Joseph. Kim insists that Dave moves in with her at Home Farm. The fight night goes ahead, but not as planned.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Emily tells Percy to move out while she has the builders in. The Platts give Don a copy of Ivy's will. Martin is put out by a condition in Ivy's will.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Derek worries about his performance review at work. Nick decides he doesn't want the house if it's going to cause problems between him and Don.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective finds himself investigating the strange and gruesome murder of an elderly psychiatrist. Elsewhere, Poirot's old friend Ariadne Oliver is being pressed to uncover the truth behind two decade-old deaths. As the pair dig deep for evidence, they soon discover that their separate investigations are linked and join forces to solve the overarching mystery. David Suchet, Zoe Wanamaker and Greta Scacchi star.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Sergeant Nokes arrives to take charge after a lorry driver is savagely beaten and his consignment of condemned meat is stolen on the way to the incinerator, causing a health alert in Aidensfield. Peggy gets involved when she is asked to house-sit Mrs Grant's mansion and takes advantage of the opportunity to run a makshift B&B, only to find herself under arrest for handling stolen goods.(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Cora's attempts at matchmaking are dashed when Mary is smitten not by intended target Evelyn Napier, but by his handsome friend, Turkish attache Kemal Pamuk. The couple's mutual attraction sparks a train of events whose consequences ripple through the entire household. Gwen is humiliated as her secret ambition is exposed, and Bates takes extreme action to cure his limp. Costume drama, starring Maggie Smith, Michelle Dockery, Brendan Patricks, Theo James, Rose Leslie and Elizabeth McGovern.(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: When a housewife is found hanged in her home, the police initially mark it down as suicide. However, a meeting with an Oxford law professor prompts a sceptical Lewis and Hathaway to believe something more sinister is afoot - a suspicion confirmed by the death of another woman. Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox star.(n)

programme.title: Bancroft
programme.desc: Free from the restrictions of her position, Elizabeth is determined to bring the true murderer to some form of justice. Armed with information to solve the case, she heads straight into the heart of the viper's nest. In a disturbing confrontation, she is forced to face her own demons. Crime thriller, starring Sarah Parish, David Avery and Charlotte Hope.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Mischievous artist Harry Field, who specialises in preparing bogus coats of arms for gullible American tourists, is murdered - and Morse takes a personal interest in finding the culprit. But as investigations progress, he discovers the victim was not quite the lovable rogue he initially appeared. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately, Geraldine James and Freddie Jones.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Arthur is confused when he comes downstairs to find PC Swirling at the front door, while Rhona brings over Johnny's guardianship documents, leaving Charity hurt. Will apologises to Harriet, and Wendy continues to seem blind to Bob's feelings for her.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Malone and Harriet have another encounter, while Al is delighted when Priya reveals she is ready to go public with their relationship, but their plans are cut short when Jai walks in on them. Cain and Moira meet to finalise their divorce, and Leanna relishes wrecking her dad's relationship.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Oscar's golfing pal asks him to investigate the disappearance of his son, who left home after a blazing row with his overbearing father and has been spotted frequenting a club in Whitby. Meanwhile, the Aidensfield police officers are baffled by the activities of a modern-day Robin Hood, who has committed a string of burglaries and given away his ill-gotten gains to the elderly residents of a new block of flats. Drama, starring Derek Fowlds.(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: First episode of the costume drama set in an Edwardian country house in 1912, written by Oscar-winning writer Julian Fellowes. The lives of the Crawley family and Downton Abbey's servants are changed for ever when the sinking of the Titanic leaves the estate without its heir and his son. The arrival of a new valet shocks the staff, who scheme to make his stay as short-lived as possible. Starring Maggie Smith, Hugh Bonneville and Elizabeth McGovern.(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The newly promoted Detective Sergeant Morse investigates when a famous international thief tries to steal a Faberge egg from an auction. The case leads to the gruesome murders of an academic and a gangster, and Morse is convinced the deaths are the work of the same man. As he struggles with his new responsibilities, he finds himself forced to mentor a younger partner (played by Poldark's Lewis Peek).(n)

programme.title: Bancroft
programme.desc: Promoted to Detective Chief Superintendent, Bancroft has professional success but at great personal cost. When a double murder brings an unexpected reunion, Elizabeth is forced onto the sidelines by Detective Superintendent Jake Harper. However, as new enemies emerge, old foes make their move. Thriller, starring Sarah Parish, David Avery, Charlotte Hope, Adam Long and Lee Boardman.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The mysterious death of a former police commissioner forces a reluctant Morse to team up with old professional rival Chief Inspector Dawson. As they and Sergeant Lewis begin to investigate, a link is uncovered to the unsolved case of a girl murdered 18 years previously. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately, Kenneth Colley and Christopher Eccleston.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: Peril at End House - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. While holidaying in Cornwall with his friend Captain Hastings, the sleuth meets a local woman who has seemingly become unaccountably accident-prone - but soon starts to suspect she is actually a victim of foul play. David Suchet and Hugh Fraser star.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: A housing shortage forces a compromise between the flatmates and their disapproving landlord. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Father Donnelly breaks some tragic news to Don. Curly, Kevin, Jim, Andy and Des form a five-a-side football team. Jack has some fun looking at expensive cars.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vera is shocked by Ivy's death. Curly signs his team up to a five-a-side league. Nick receives a letter from Ivy in the event of her death which contains some stunning news.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Passions run high as Frank finally confronts Kim and Dave.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim attempts to convince Frank that the baby is his and not Dave's. Frank spends the night at Zoe's and feels like an old fool. Mandy tries to flirt with Dave in the tearoom.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Oscar's golfing pal asks him to investigate the disappearance of his son, who left home after a blazing row with his overbearing father and has been spotted frequenting a club in Whitby. Meanwhile, the Aidensfield police officers are baffled by the activities of a modern-day Robin Hood, who has committed a string of burglaries and given away his ill-gotten gains to the elderly residents of a new block of flats. Drama, starring Derek Fowlds.(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.sub-title: Climb the Greasy Pole - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. Pop and Ma become the target of dirty tricks and smear tactics during the election campaign, and Charley is placed in a compromising position by Abigail's teacher.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Jo try to foil a showdown at the Mucky Duck Saloon when Robin and loud Irishman Mick get into an argument. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Newlyweds Mike and Ellie decide to build their dream home at a beauty spot called Gypsy's Acre, ignoring the warnings of elusive romany Mrs Lee about an ancient curse supposedly cast upon the land. The couple settle into their new life, but the mysterious woman makes her presence felt, subjecting them to a campaign of hatred in an attempt to force them out. Tragedy soon strikes, but as luck would have it Miss Marple is staying nearby with her recently widowed old friend Mrs Phillpot. However, if the spinster sleuth is to solve the puzzle, she must put her own life in grave danger. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with Wendy Craig, Glynis Barber, Hugh Dennis, Tamzin Outhwaite, Tom Hughes, Joanna Vanderham, Aneurin Barnard, William Hope and Janet Henfrey.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A disgruntled client falls under suspicion when an insurance salesman is found murdered, but the victim's complicated private life gives Mason a lot to think about before the case is solved. Alf Ventress makes himself at home in the Aidensfield Arms while his house undergoes repairs, and soon overstays his welcome, while a drunk locked up in the cells for a night turns out to be a war hero friend of Sgt Miller fallen on hard times.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Linda tells Viv that she and Biff are no longer together. Frank makes it clear that as soon as Kim is well he wants her out. Dave sneaks into Kim's bedroom.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Ned and Roy are amused to see Zak jogging. Kim has no intention of leaving Home Farm with nothing and calls her solicitor. Jack and Sarah are fed up with looking after Joseph.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Nick agrees with Gail and Martin not to say anything about Ivy's letter. At the funeral tea, Vera snaps and tells Josie she shouldn't be in Ivy's house.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jack is horrified to learn that his inheritance makes him ineligible for claiming social. The Platts try to make Nick see that Ivy's letter isn't a will and might not be valid.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective's friend Sir Charles Cartwright (guest star Martin Shaw) hosts a cocktail party at his home in Cornwall, where an amiable local priest chokes to death on his drink. There seems to be no motive for murder, and no trace of poison found in the victim's glass, but weeks later, when Sir Charles's doctor friend Sir Bartholomew Strange dies in similar circumstances, he and Poirot team up to investigate. Drama, with Art Malik, Jane Asher and Anna Carteret, and David Suchet in the title role.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A disgruntled client falls under suspicion when an insurance salesman is found murdered, but the victim's complicated private life gives Mason a lot to think about before the case is solved. Alf Ventress makes himself at home in the Aidensfield Arms while his house undergoes repairs, and soon overstays his welcome, while a drunk locked up in the cells for a night turns out to be a war hero friend of Sgt Miller fallen on hard times.(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Everyone is anxious at the arrival of Matthew Crawley, the heir presumptive, but he soon encounters problems settling into his new life in Downton, and his mother Isobel immediately crosses swords with Violet. Meanwhile, butler Carson is mortified to be confronted with his past, and housemaid Gwen seems to have a secret correspondent. Costume drama, starring Maggie Smith, Hugh Bonneville, Dan Stevens, Penelope Wilton, Elizabeth McGovern, Jim Carter and Rose Leslie.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: John Barnaby arrives in Midsomer and the headmistress of an upmarket girls' boarding school wastes no time in asking him to patrol a forthcoming classic-car show. However, when the local DJ who was set to judge the vehicles is crushed to death, the detective is soon investigating his first murder case since replacing cousin Tom - and as the death toll rises, he finds himself in a perilous situation. Drama, guest starring David Warner and Samantha Bond, with Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes.(n)

programme.title: Bancroft
programme.desc: As the murder evidence begins to build, Harper and the team are met with conflicting accounts. Meanwhile, Elizabeth finds herself taunted by one of the suspects and the trouble around the Kamara deal, forcing her to choose between her personal and professional loyalties. As the chief superintendent is deserted by her allies, she enters a dangerous game of cat and mouse. Crime thriller, starring Sarah Parish, David Avery and Charlotte Hope.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The fatal poisoning of a deaconess who was about to become Oxford's first female chaplain makes Morse wonder whether the city's conservative clerics could be hiding a deadly secret. Further investigation uncovers a connection between the murder and a weight loss clinic. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately and Zoe Wanamaker (My Family).(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: Peril at End House - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Belgian sleuth's Cornish holiday comes to a dramatic conclusion as he unravels the mystery behind the series of disastrous near-misses that placed the life of a beautiful heiress in danger, and as the truth comes to light, a secret engagement is revealed. David Suchet stars, with Philip Jackson and Hugh Fraser.(n)

programme.desc: Chrissy and Jo try to foil a showdown at the Mucky Duck Saloon when Robin and loud Irishman Mick get into an argument. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: The season of goodwill descends on the Larkin household, and the family celebrates the 'perfick' Christmas. Drama, based on the characters created by HE Bates, starring David Jason, Pam Ferris and Catherine Zeta-Jones.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin and Jo become concerned when Chrissy starts dating an older man - so they decide to play gooseberry. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective's friend Sir Charles Cartwright (guest star Martin Shaw) hosts a cocktail party at his home in Cornwall, where an amiable local priest chokes to death on his drink. There seems to be no motive for murder, and no trace of poison found in the victim's glass, but weeks later, when Sir Charles's doctor friend Sir Bartholomew Strange dies in similar circumstances, he and Poirot team up to investigate. Drama, with Art Malik, Jane Asher and Anna Carteret, and David Suchet in the title role.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Sergeant Nokes arrives to take charge after a lorry driver is savagely beaten and his consignment of condemned meat is stolen on the way to the incinerator, causing a health alert in Aidensfield. Peggy gets involved when she is asked to house-sit Mrs Grant's mansion and takes advantage of the opportunity to run a makshift B&B, only to find herself under arrest for handling stolen goods.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sarah panics when Chris takes Joseph. Kim insists that Dave moves in with her at Home Farm. The fight night goes ahead, but not as planned.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Emily tells Percy to move out while she has the builders in. The Platts give Don a copy of Ivy's will. Martin is put out by a condition in Ivy's will.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Derek worries about his performance review at work. Nick decides he doesn't want the house if it's going to cause problems between him and Don.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective finds himself investigating the strange and gruesome murder of an elderly psychiatrist. Elsewhere, Poirot's old friend Ariadne Oliver is being pressed to uncover the truth behind two decade-old deaths. As the pair dig deep for evidence, they soon discover that their separate investigations are linked and join forces to solve the overarching mystery. David Suchet, Zoe Wanamaker and Greta Scacchi star.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Sergeant Nokes arrives to take charge after a lorry driver is savagely beaten and his consignment of condemned meat is stolen on the way to the incinerator, causing a health alert in Aidensfield. Peggy gets involved when she is asked to house-sit Mrs Grant's mansion and takes advantage of the opportunity to run a makshift B&B, only to find herself under arrest for handling stolen goods.(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Cora's attempts at matchmaking are dashed when Mary is smitten not by intended target Evelyn Napier, but by his handsome friend, Turkish attache Kemal Pamuk. The couple's mutual attraction sparks a train of events whose consequences ripple through the entire household. Gwen is humiliated as her secret ambition is exposed, and Bates takes extreme action to cure his limp. Costume drama, starring Maggie Smith, Michelle Dockery, Brendan Patricks, Theo James, Rose Leslie and Elizabeth McGovern.(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: When a housewife is found hanged in her home, the police initially mark it down as suicide. However, a meeting with an Oxford law professor prompts a sceptical Lewis and Hathaway to believe something more sinister is afoot - a suspicion confirmed by the death of another woman. Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox star.(n)

programme.title: Bancroft
programme.desc: Free from the restrictions of her position, Elizabeth is determined to bring the true murderer to some form of justice. Armed with information to solve the case, she heads straight into the heart of the viper's nest. In a disturbing confrontation, she is forced to face her own demons. Crime thriller, starring Sarah Parish, David Avery and Charlotte Hope.(n)

programme.title: Strike It Lucky
programme.desc: Michael Barrymore hosts the madcap game show, in which three teams of two prove seek to progress along a board game-style path by answering questions. Each duo are allowed to determine how many spaces they would like to move by answering the corresponding number of questions. Landing on a space reveals it's hidden contents, which could amount to a prize, or a 'hot spot', which causes contestants to forfeit their gathered winnings.(n)

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Moriarty seeks revenge on Holmes for foiling his plan to sell the Mona Lisa, and the pair ultimately fight their final battle at the Reichenbach Falls in Switzerland. Jeremy Brett stars as the titular hero, with David Burke making his final appearance as Dr Watson and Eric Porter as Moriarty.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur buys a large consignment of wine, supposedly brewed by monks. Before he can collect his order it is impounded, but he sneaks into a warehouse and retrieves it anyway. However, a disastrous tasting session at the Winchester reveals the product to be 'corked' and undrinkable and when he and Ray return it in the hope of a later refund they get locked in. George Cole and Gary Webster star.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle infiltrate a gang smuggling art treasures and industrial secrets out of the country and selling them on to foreign powers. As the investigation proceeds, the duo discover the illicit operation is being run by a country squire in his stately home. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A routine surveillance assignment leads Regan and Carter to a massive car-parking fraud. Crackerjack veteran Peter Glaze features in the supporting cast, alongside John Thaw and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: A woman suffering from post-traumatic amnesia is pushed down an escalator at a job fair and Jack discovers an assassin has targeted her.(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: A down-on-his-luck cop with a gambling problem ends up owing money to a gangster - who offers to clear the debt if he kills Crocker. Guest starring Judith Light (Ugly Betty).(n)

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: The great detective reveals to a startled Watson how he defeated Moriarty and cheated death beneath the waters of the Reichenbach Falls. Period drama, marking Edward Hardwicke's debut as Holmes' indefatigable chronicler.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur's other half, 'er Indoors, disappears and he finds himself under suspicion of burying her on the forecourt in order to use the insurance money to prop up his dubious business ventures. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: A recently released convict causes chaos when he takes a group of hospital patients hostage. Bodie and Doyle try to get to him before he can start shooting. Drama, starring Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: An antiques shop is used as a point of access in a bank robbery, and it looks like an inside job, but Regan suspects otherwise and soon finds himself on a collision course with Haskins. Police drama, starring John Thaw, Dennis Waterman and Garfield Morgan.(n)

programme.title: Brian Moore Meets
programme.desc: A classic 1979 interview with the former striker during his time at Hamburg SV. Keegan had recently been crowned European Footballer of the Year, and cameras reveal how he was ahead of his time in terms of realising his commercial potential.(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade visits southern Africa to see if he can track down a deadly fish that lurks in the eerie waters of the Okavango. A killer with shark-like teeth, there are stories of it hunting in packs and feeding on schooling fish or even human bodies. Jeremy is determined to find out if it is a giant piranha or something equally terrifying.(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade takes on Africa's wild Zambezi River to find an unknown predator dragging fishermen to their deaths. Crocodiles and hippos are guilty of killing dozens of victims in plain view, yet it is said there is another monstrous beast stalking these deep and turbulent waters. Locals speak of 'the Mazunda' - but what is this sinister, child-swallowing creature?(n)

programme.title: The Equalizer (2014)
programme.desc: A retired secret agent applies his talents to the task of helping victims of injustice. When he witnesses a prostitute being beaten up by a pimp, he kills the man, an act that draws him into a battle to the death with a Russian crime syndicate. Action thriller based on the 1980s TV series, starring Denzel Washington and Chloe Grace Moretz.(n)

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: The youthful heir to a fortune disappears from boarding school in the dead of night - but his aristocratic father's aversion to publicity makes the case particularly difficult to investigate. Period drama, starring Jeremy Brett.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur is conned into buying an East German car, a retired greyhound and a piano. He fears the items could destroy his reputation, leaving him with the job of selling them on without the rest of the manor finding out. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: When a foreign student, an apparent cocaine trafficker, is shot dead, Cowley tasks Bodie and Doyle with targetting teenage traffickers who have diplomatic ties with South America. Drama, starring Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins, Gordon Jackson and Alice Krige.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A trusted informant tips Regan off about an upcoming robbery, but the gang involved kidnaps his daughter to blackmail him into taking no action. Police drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.title: Brian Moore Meets
programme.desc: A classic 1979 interview with the Formula One legend, who survived a life-threatening crash in 1976 and returned just three weeks later. The programme provides a revealing portrait of one of motorsport's most respected figures, who died last year.(n)

programme.title: Junk and Disorderly
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Sam Lovegrove look to make a profit on motorcycle memorabilia.(n)

programme.title: The Gauntlet (1977)
programme.desc: A downbeat cop sets out to escort a prostitute across America so she can give evidence in a high-profile Mob trial. However, corrupt officials are bent on ensuring the witness never makes it to court, and subject the pair to a series of attacks. Meanwhile, the mismatched duo's bid to reach their destination alive is complicated by a burgeoning romance. Action adventure, directed by and starring Clint Eastwood. With Sondra Locke, Bill McKinney and Pat Hingle.(n)

programme.title: Senna (2010)
programme.desc: Documentary about the life of Brazilian Formula One driver Ayrton Senna, examining his achievements on the track over a 10-year period that saw him crowned world champion three times, until his death in a crash during the 1994 San Marino Grand Prix. Featuring rare footage from the F1 archives and contributions from fellow racing stars including Alain Prost and Jackie Stewart.(n)

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: The mismatched duo investigate the mysterious death of a famous singer's bodyguard. Meanwhile, Riggs learns about the responsibilities that come with being in a relationship - with help from Murtaugh - as he and Palmer struggle in their blossoming romance. Clayne Crawford and Damon Wayans star.(n)

programme.desc: The coroner is outraged when he learns that a wealthy lawyer is likely to get away with a light sentence after running over a pedestrian.(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Kojak does not approve of a female detective joining his team, but they must work together to bring in a murderous drug dealer. Guest starring Joan Van Ark (Knots Landing).(n)

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: The prime minister asks Holmes to investigate the disappearance of a letter which could lead to a major European catastrophe. Unfortunately, completion of the task requires him to jeopardise a titled lady's reputation - something he is naturally reluctant to do. Victorian detective drama, starring Jeremy Brett.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur spies a business opportunity as he and Ray prepare to pay their respects to the late Charlie Johnson, but the deceased's widow has other ideas. Comedy drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: A notorious hard-nosed villain offers to co-operate with the police and gives himself up in return for protection for his wife and their children, so Bodie and Doyle are assigned to perform the task. Crime drama, starring Lewis Collins, Martin Shaw and Gordon Jackson.(n)

programme.title: Banks of England
programme.desc: A celebration of the life of former England goalkeeper Gordon Banks, who won the World Cup in 1966, and was also remembered for a sensational save from Pele in the tournament four years later. Featuring insights from past and present players, as well as Banks himself.(n)

programme.title: Euro 96 Relived
programme.desc: Netherlands v England. A chance to see the Group A encounter at Wembley Stadium. The Dutch side contained the likes of Dennis Bergkamp, Clarence Seedorf and Edwin van der Sar, but it was Teddy Sheringham and Alan Shearer who took centre stage as England produced one of their finest-ever performances. Bob Wilson presented the coverage alongside John Barnes and Jack Charlton.(n)

programme.title: Sudden Death (1995)
programme.desc: Terrorists seize control of an ice-hockey stadium in the middle of a match attended by the US vice president. Taking the politician hostage, they threaten to blow up the building at the final whistle - but a resourceful former fireman takes it upon himself to thwart their dastardly plot. Action thriller, starring Jean-Claude Van Damme, Powers Boothe, Raymond J Barry and Whittni Wright.(n)

programme.desc: As the country prepares for the easing of lockdown, this documentary tells the stories of the elderly and vulnerable people self-isolating during the coronavirus crisis, for whom lockdown may continue for many more months. Featuring people from all over the nation and filmed on location, their stories convey the immense challenge of being especially vulnerable in the midst of a pandemic. Suzanne, who has terminal cancer, has come up with a novel approach to seeing her friends, while Desmond has been forced to move in with his daughter and granddaughter to avoid the virus, which has already taken the lives of at least four of his friends.(n)

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Rajiv is devastated when he loses his crown, thinking that it's the only reason he is any good at anything. However, that night Noor Jehan's brakes fail, and Rajiv saves the day as she is about to crash into Shankar, discovering that he can be a useful engine without his crown after all.(n)

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.sub-title: Thomas & Friends: Big World! Big Adventures!

programme.title: Top Wing

programme.title: Sunny Bunnies

programme.title: Jeremy Vine

programme.desc: After taking pity on a single mother, landlord Ronnie discovered mystery occupants have turned his property into a scrap metal merchants. Brent Council housing enforcement officer Herb discovers 26 labourers living in life-threatening conditions in a single house, while letting agent John tries to find a solution for a tenant living in squalor due to an issue with hoarding.(n)

programme.title: 5 News at Lunchtime
programme.desc: Round-up of the day's headlines from around the world.(n)

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Blanche suspects she may be pregnant, but the truth is that she is beginning her menopause.(n)

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Dorothy has a hot date with a sailor, but the evening is threatened when her ex-husband Stan turns up.(n)

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Willow still struggles with her emotions, but is grateful for Dean and Colby's support. Dean reluctantly gets involved in Mackenzie's love life when he sees her flirting with Ari.(n)

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: The Kennedys find out the result of the custody ruling, while Dee brings up Andrea's request for a photograph of Hugo with Toadie.(n)

programme.title: A Dangerous Date (2018)
programme.desc: A woman introduces her single father to the world of online dating, and when he meets Alexis, it looks like love at first sight for Peter. However, daughter Julie suspects there might be more to his new flame than meets the eye. Thriller, starring Jillian Murray, David Chokachi and Brianna Joy Chomer.(n)

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel reveals who the father of her baby is, while Chandler and Monica go on their honeymoon, but are constantly upstaged by another couple who get preferential treatment wherever they go.(n)

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross and Rachel's attempt to explain how they ended up in bed together descends into an argument about who made the first move - until Ross shocks everyone by announcing he captured the whole encounter on videotape. David Schwimmer and Jennifer Aniston star.(n)

programme.title: 5 News at 5
programme.desc: Round-up of the day's headlines from around the world.(n)

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: The Kennedys find out the result of the custody ruling, while Dee brings up Andrea's request for a photograph of Hugo with Toadie.(n)

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Willow still struggles with her emotions, but is grateful for Dean and Colby's support. Dean reluctantly gets involved in Mackenzie's love life when he sees her flirting with Ari.(n)

programme.title: 5 News Tonight
programme.desc: A round-up of the evening's headlines, including coverage of the latest national and international stories.(n)

programme.title: Wonderful World of Chocolate
programme.desc: Documentary offering a glimpse inside the confectionery world, visiting factories, chocolatiers and shops, and even revealing the Queen's favourite chocolates.(n)

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Chris travels along the route of the Soviet-era Trans-Caucasus Railway, beginning in the Azerbaijani capital of Baku. He then heads into Georgia, exploring Tbilisi's vintage metro system and funicular railway, before travelling to the holiday resort of Borjomi, where world-class engineering is on display in a mountain rail bridge designed by Gustave Eiffel.(n)

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: In Derby, Nick needs urgent back-up as a reckless driver tries to avoid capture by ramming his police car, while Alex races to back up colleague John in dealing with an erratic driver who turns out to be carrying a knife.(n)

programme.title: Casualty 24/7
programme.desc: A toddler is rushed in with suspected sepsis, a life-threatening condition that attacks the organs, and a large team of specialists administer high-strength antibiotics. Elsewhere, advanced nurse practitioner Cheryl is looking after 57-year-old Michael, who has come in with severe abdominal pain, and an 87-year-old woman is rushed in after being knocked over by a car.(n)

programme.title: And Proud
programme.desc: Documentary examining the lifestyles of some of those living on benefits who are not currently seeking employment, including a Gloucestershire mother-of-11 who receives benefits equivalent to three times the typical UK family income. Also featured are a Liverpool couple who have not worked for six years, and two single mums in Camden whose rents are more than £1,200 a month each and are paid by the state.(n)

programme.title: The Sex Business
programme.sub-title: Swinging Both Ways: The Sex Business
programme.desc: The documentary explores the lives of straight sex workers who have turned their heterosexual predilections upside down to perform in gay roles for money. A cammer and porn star reveal what it takes to sell sex to fellow performers of the same gender, despite being straight in their private lives.(n)

programme.title: BSL Zone
programme.desc: The inside story of the characters and narrative of HERE/NOT HERE, the deaf hip hop music drama.(n)

programme.title: When Eight Bells Toll (1971)
programme.desc: A Navy intelligence agent heads to Scotland, where he poses as a marine biologist to investigate the theft of millions of pounds in gold bullion from ships off the British coast. However, the locals counter his enquiries with hostility, especially when he sets his sights on a Greek tycoon, whom he suspects may be operating as a modern-day pirate. Thriller, with Anthony Hopkins, Robert Morley and Jack Hawkins. Based on a novel by Alistair MacLean.(n)

programme.title: Cottage to Let (1941)
programme.desc: A British inventor's revolutionary bombsight is set to have a positive impact on the war effort. However, military intelligence detects the Nazis are aware of the invention, while a plucky teenage cockney evacuated to the boffin's Scottish country estate sets out to expose a German spy ring. Second World War thriller, with Leslie Banks, John Mills, Alastair Sim and George Cole in his screen debut.(n)

programme.title: Campbell's Kingdom (1957)
programme.desc: A man who has only six months to live returns to the land his family owns in the Canadian Rockies. He intends to prove his grandfather's frequently derided theory that there is oil to be found there, but he has little time to find it, as the area will be flooded when construction is completed on a nearby dam. Adventure, with Dirk Bogarde, Stanley Baker, Michael Craig and Barbara Murray.(n)

programme.title: Destroyer (1943)
programme.desc: A former welder working on the Navy destroyer whose construction he oversaw angers his crewmates with his relentless quest for perfection. Wartime adventure, starring Edward G Robinson, Glenn Ford, Marguerite Chapman and Regis Toomey.(n)

programme.title: The Maze Runner (2014)
programme.desc: A teen arrives in an isolated community of youngsters, with no memory of who he is or the outside world. When another new arrival brings an ominous message, he realises time is running out for the group and they must find an escape route through a deadly labyrinth if they are to survive. Sci-fi adventure based on James Dashner's novel, starring Dylan O'Brien, Kaya Scodelario and Patricia Clarkson.(n)

programme.title: Split (2016)
programme.desc: After being abducted by a kidnapper with multiple personalities, three young women are soon engaged in a fight for survival. Meanwhile, an oblivious psychiatrist tries to ease her patient's fractured mind as a 24th character with apparent superhuman abilities attempts to dominate his body. M Night Shyamalan's thriller, starring James McAvoy, Anya Taylor-Joy and Betty Buckley.(n)

programme.title: The Warrior (2001)
programme.desc: A warrior vows to give up his violent ways after a crisis of conscience, but his master is left fuming at this unexpected change of heart, and sends hired killers to exact a suitably bloody revenge. A trek through the Himalayas ensues, as the former champion sets out to find inner peace - with his pursuers hot on his tail. Historical drama, starring Irrfan Khan, Puru Chhibber and Sheikh Annuddin. In Hindi.(n)

programme.title: A Taxi Driver (2017)
programme.desc: A taxi driver from Seoul unintentionally becomes involved in the events of the Gwangju Uprising in 1980. Jang Hoon's drama, starring Song Kang-ho, Thomas Kretschmann and Yoo Hae-jin. In Korean, English and German.(n)

programme.title: Three Faces West (1940)
programme.desc: An Austrian doctor and his daughter arrive in the dust bowl regions of America after fleeing the Nazis, and join the exodus of farmers seeking fertile land in Oregon. The daughter falls for the pioneer leading the journey, despite being engaged to the man who helped her escape the Third Reich. Drama, starring John Wayne, Charles Coburn and Sigrid Gurie.(n)

programme.title: The Million Pound Note (1954)
programme.desc: An eccentric multimillionaire bets his equally rich brother that if a man is known to be wealthy, he can obtain everything he needs on credit. To put this theory to the test, they give a £1million note to a penniless seaman, but instruct him never to spend it. Comedy adapted from a story by Mark Twain, starring Gregory Peck, Jane Griffiths, Joyce Grenfell and Wilfrid Hyde White.(n)

programme.title: Tiger Bay (1959)
programme.desc: A Polish sailor flees Cardiff's docklands after murdering his girlfriend in a jealous rage, initially unaware that a 12-year-old tomboy has witnessed the crime and has taken his abandoned gun to show off to her friends. However, he comes back to abduct the girl in a bid to keep her quiet - but the pair form a friendship and she tries to help him escape the police. Thriller, starring Hayley Mills in her film debut alongside her father John, with Horst Buchholz.(n)

programme.title: The Lady Vanishes (1979)
programme.desc: An American heiress befriends an elderly lady on a continental train journey, but wakes one morning to find she has disappeared and the other passengers deny having ever seen the missing woman - leading to suspicions that a conspiracy is afoot. Remake of Hitchcock's 1938 mystery, starring Elliott Gould, Cybill Shepherd, Angela Lansbury, Herbert Lom, Arthur Lowe and Ian Carmichael.(n)

programme.title: Warcraft (2016)
programme.desc: A kingdom stands on the brink of war when it is invaded by a vast horde of creatures who have travelled through a portal from their dying world. As dark magic works its influence on the world, two leaders from either side are set on a collision course with each other as they strive to protect their people. Fantasy adventure based on the best-selling computer game, starring Travis Fimmel, Paula Patton and Ben Foster.(n)

programme.title: The Grand Budapest Hotel (2014)
programme.desc: An old man tells a journalist the story of his youth as a lobby boy at a hotel in Eastern Europe in the 1930s. He was taken under the wing of the establishment's dashing concierge, and is drawn into his misadventures as he becomes involved in a conflict over a valuable painting. Wes Anderson's comedy drama, starring Ralph Fiennes, Tony Revolori, F Murray Abraham and Jude Law.(n)

programme.title: My Friend Dahmer (2017)
programme.desc: The early years of Ohio serial killer Jeffrey Dahmer are charted in this drama inspired by his friend Derf Backderf's graphic novel, starring Ross Lynch, Anne Heche and Alex Wolff.(n)

programme.title: Mona Lisa (1986)
programme.desc: A downtrodden small-time crook gets a job as bodyguard and chauffeur to an emotionally bruised call girl, whom he gradually falls in love with. She in turn persuades him to help her save a teenage prostitute from her pimp, a goal that leads to conflict with a brutal gangster. Romantic crime thriller, starring Bob Hoskins, Cathy Tyson, Michael Caine, Robbie Coltrane and Kate Hardie.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals (1966)
programme.desc: A wealthy Texan hires four mercenaries to rescue his wife, who has been abducted and taken to Mexico by a bandit gang. However, when they find her, she refuses to leave and they have to take her by force - ending in a cross-border chase with the outlaws on their trail. Western adventure, with Burt Lancaster, Lee Marvin, Robert Ryan, Woody Strode, Jack Palance and Claudia Cardinale.(n)

programme.title: How Green Was My Valley (1941)
programme.desc: Residents of a Welsh mining community at the start of the 20th century struggle to come to terms with the decline in their traditional way of life. Oscar-winning drama, starring Walter Pidgeon, Maureen O'Hara, Donald Crisp and Roddy McDowall.(n)

programme.title: Gallipoli (1981)
programme.desc: Two patriotic Australians join the army to fight during the First World War. However, their youthful idealism is quickly eroded by the harsh reality of the conflict, and their friendship is tested to the very limits of endurance as they prepare for combat ahead of the pivotal battle of Gallipoli in Turkey. Drama, directed by Peter Weir, starring Mel Gibson, Mark Lee, Bill Kerr and David Argue.(n)

programme.title: A Beautiful Mind (2001)
programme.desc: Biopic of the brilliant American mathematician John Nash, whose prospects of a promising career with the CIA were confounded by paranoid delusions and schizophrenia as the stress of the job proved too much of a burden. Drama, starring Russell Crowe, Jennifer Connelly, Ed Harris, Judd Hirsch, Paul Bettany and Christopher Plummer.(n)

programme.title: The Martian (2015)
programme.desc: A manned mission to Mars is abruptly abandoned, with one crew member left for dead. However, the astronaut has survived and faces a series of challenges - to contact Earth and arrange his rescue and to survive for the years it will take for a spacecraft to reach him on resources intended to last the crew a month. Ridley Scott's sci-fi drama based on the novel by Andy Weir, starring Matt Damon, Jessica Chastain, Kristen Wiig and Chiwetel Ejiofor.(n)

programme.title: The Daughter (2015)
programme.desc: A man returning home for his father's wedding uncovers a secret that threatens to destroy the lives of those he left behind. Drama, with Paul Schneider and Geoffrey Rush.(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Joe plans to admit his feelings for Mel after their romantic night in New Jersey, but is stopped when he finds out her ex-boyfriend Austin wants to give the relationship a second chance. Meanwhile, Ryder makes himself sick through his own cooking.(n)

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Ben discovers a shocking secret about Riley's new love Fitch, while things heat up between Danny and girlfriend Milena, and Tucker seizes an opportunity to impress his boss Chase. Comedy, starring Jean-Luc Bilodeau.(n)

Meanwhile, on the International Space Station, Howard is being bullied by the other astronauts. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco.(n)

programme.desc: Beverly tries to help Adam navigate a painless path through puberty, but just as his voice is changing, he is cast in the lead role of the school musical. Meanwhile, Murray is irked when Erica chooses musician Johnny over Barry's sidekick Geoff as her date for the homecoming dance.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Barry accidentally records over one of Beverly's prized family videotapes - her treasured keepsake from his fifth birthday party. Meanwhile, Adam and Murray build a robot together, but the project comes between them when they experience creative differences.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: When Jeffords becomes anxious about his upcoming lieutenant's exam, Peralta, Captain Holt and the squad decide to distract him from the stress by holding the precinct's annual Halloween Heist on a new date, Cinco de Mayo.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Peralta and Boyle investigate a serial killer who poses a severe danger to the public. Captain Holt continues to spar with his rival, Commissioner Kelly. Jeffords must deal with some complications resulting from his recent news about his lieutenant's exam.(n)

programme.desc: Howard has a meeting with Colonel Williams, a representative from the Air Force Department of Material Command, about the gyroscope he invented with Leonard and Sheldon. Penny accidentally reveals that Bernadette is pregnant to their colleagues at work. Dean Norris guest stars.(n)

Raj learns Bernadette's true feelings about her pregnancy when he helps her to clear out the spare room for a nursery.(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Mel and Joe volunteer at the school dance, but get themselves kicked out by the school's vice principal for bad behaviour. Meanwhile, Ryder considers breaking up with Stella. Family comedy, starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Mel takes Joe's side when he is involved in a hit-and-run accident, until she discovers that the other driver is her beloved hairdresser Calista. Meanwhile, Ryder tries to win Stella back during lessons, much to the frustration of their teacher. Starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Ben offers to dog-sit for his girlfriend Megan, but the animal does not seem to like him much. Elsewhere, Tucker is angered by his room-mates' messy habits, so Danny hires someone to clean the apartment. Comedy, starring Jean-Luc Bilodeau.(n)

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Ben persuades Bonnie and Ray to invite his conservative Aunt Betty to Emma's baptism in the hope she will give the baby a generous gift, but this means hiding Ray's sexuality from her. Meanwhile, Riley makes Tucker and Danny compete to be chosen as the godfather.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake's dreams come true when he gets the chance to spend the day with his hero, veteran crime reporter Jimmy Brogan, but their encounter has unexpected consequences. Meanwhile, Terry and Charles prepare Rosa for a day in court, teaching her to be less terrifying on the witness stand. Guest starring Stacy Keach.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: When Jake's favourite pizzeria burns down, the fire marshal (Patton Oswalt) believes the owner should be the prime suspect in the investigation. Outraged, the detective does everything in his power to prove him wrong, even if it means stepping outside his jurisdiction. Meanwhile, chaos ensues at the precinct after a computer virus publishes everyone's internet browser history.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: In an attempt to get closer to Barry and Erica, Beverly promises to bring actor Scott Baio to town for a big charity event. Adam gets into a tricky situation when he arranges to take both his best friend Emmy and his girlfriend Dana to the school dance.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: After watching a particular game show together for years, Adam and Emmy are excited to learn that the producers will be holding auditions at their school. Beverly turns to Bill for help when Barry and Murray become convinced that she is a lucky charm for their beleaguered football team.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: After spending the holidays apart, the gang get together to hear about Sheldon's and Amy's trip to his mother's, Leonard and Penny recall their Christmas tree adventure, and Howard and Bernadette open up about their first days as parents.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny thinks that Leonard is taking her for granted and has stopped making an effort in their relationship, so she decides to go away for a spa weekend with Amy. Howard and Raj go to extreme lengths to deal with a squeaky floorboard in the baby's bedroom.(n)

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One that Went off the Rails
programme.desc: Self-conscious groom-to-be Richard plans a first-class trip for his straight-talking bride Latrice on her wedding day.(n)

programme.title: The Inbetweeners Movie (2011)
programme.desc: Awkward teenagers Simon, Will, Jay and Neil plan a holiday to the Greek island of Crete after they finish their exams, hoping to experience drink, sex and debauchery beyond their wildest dreams. However, things do not quite work out as planned. Comedy based on the TV series, with Joe Thomas, Simon Bird, James Buckley and Blake Harrison.(n)

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Two more singletons choose a date from a selection of six naked people, who will be revealed to them one body part at a time. After nine months of being single, 23-year-old university graduate Sapphire from Kent has decided to see if she can find her ideal woman by stripping back the layers. Thirty-two-year-old account manager Kieron from Basingstoke has spent the past three years single to focus on his career, but now he's ready to put finding a partner at the top of his to do list. Anna Richardson hosts.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard isn't thrilled when he finds out Penny is considering taking a new job that has been offered to her by her former boyfriend Zack. Sheldon attempts to develop his brain further by learning new skills.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Amy is offered a visiting research fellowship at Princeton University, and Raj makes some new living arrangements. When the guys complete their infinite persistence gyroscope project, events spin out of control.(n)

programme.title: Made in Chelsea
programme.desc: Cheska tries to cheer Binky up by arranging a glitzy girls' night out. Andy struggles to cut ties with Louise, and Francis spots them together at a bookshop. Meanwhile, two new girls catch the boys' eyes, but Jamie does not know what he is letting himself in for when he secures a date with one of them.(n)

programme.title: Gogglebox

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: After being stood up the previous week, Geordie model Georgia ended up flirting with nice guy Alex. Now they are back to pick up where they left off. Baker Chloe prides herself on being a good judge of character, which is just as well because her date, amateur dramatics enthusiast Ben, has played many different roles in his time. Swinging sixties fan Robert is hoping fifties fashionista Amy will accompany him to a Northern Soul night after their meal - although he might have second thoughts when he sees what's tattooed on her toe. Plus, the subject of family life proves to be high on the agenda for make-up artist Verona and fire-fighter Paul, as well as father-of-one Patrick and German-born Thomas.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine

Doctors are concerned it may not be pumping enough blood around her body, and quickly prescribe fast-acting medication. programme.desc: The competition begins at Chimneys B&B in Holbrook, Suffolk, where owner Carolyn Spilling hopes her upbeat personality will be a hit with the guests.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: At Copperfields Guest House in Broadstairs, Kent, owners Neale and Julie Taylor hope their exclusively vegetarian B&B will stand out from the crowd.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at The George and Abbotsford in Melrose, Scotland, where Andria Gardner and John Marr aim to pull out all the stops to impress their guests.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests spend the night at Massimo Perrulli's hotel in County Durham, where one couple have cleanliness concerns, another are missing a bath and the third have an axe to grind.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet to find out what they have been paid and settle some scores. Chimneys B&B's Carolyn and her friend Delia want some home truths from Massimo and Raf.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien search for more gems hidden among unwanted possessions, trying to make a profit from church organ pipes and old leather sports car seats.(n)

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Veteran actors and married couple Timothy West and Prunella Scales continue their journey and reach the Rochdale Canal - a waterway that was a main artery for the textile industry at the height of the Industrial Revolution. They begin in Sowerby Bridge, West Yorkshire, and head west through Mytholmroyd, before journeying up over the Pennines to the canal summit, more than 600ft above sea level.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Debbie and Nigel start landscaping their five-acre grounds and risk a flat-pack fall out. Also requiring urgent attention is the tired roof terrace at the rear of the chateau which currently isn't watertight. Sarah and George run into trouble with the chateau's electrics as they host their first-ever wedding. Dick gets to work re-levelling the chateau's long driveway and Stephanie turns her attention to a new, modern apartment in the chateau's old stables.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs Abroad
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud visits Malaga, where he follows the progress of English architect Gil Briffa and wife Hilary. The couple have to put their trust in local Spanish builders to construct their vision of a dream contemporary home in the heart of a sleepy Andalusian community.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A teenager is rushed to hospital with a lacerated wrist after putting his hand through a pane of glass, an incident which leaves his father reflecting on his family history. Another patient discusses being diagnosed with a chronic condition that causes constant stomach pain, and a 76-year-old woman suffers severe shortness of breath and a tight chest.(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: In Birmingham, paramedics Paul Johnson and Maddie Murray are called out in the early hours of the morning to a 60-year-old patient with severe chest pains.(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show's version of the words and numbers quiz. Guest captain Richard Ayoade is joined by Rob Beckett, going up against Jon Richardson and Claudia Winkleman, with Adam Riches accompanying Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner and Rachel Riley dishing out the all-important consonants, vowels and numbers.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Boca Raton in Florida to turn around the fortunes of Italian restaurant Anna Vincenzo's. The owner opened the establishment in 2001 with the help of her father but the business has failed to meet her expectations, and the chef realises that her ruthless style of management is having an adverse influence, not only on her staff but also on her family.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Another chance to see some of the programme's more memorable investigations, including Kate Quilton's investigation into saffron fraud in the Atlas Mountains, Morocco, Mark Tebbutt's exploration of how glace cherries are made and Jimmy Doherty's revelation into what pub grub favourite whitebait actually is.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp enlists the help of seamstress Lisa Comfort to help Amber and Colin create a memory quilt. However, Amber's lack of ironing skills could result in disaster.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Deborah Sims has long dreamt of relocating permanently to southern Spain. With a budget of £90,000, she is looking for a property in the Manilva region, with an easy commute to Gibraltar. It's a big lifestyle change to make on her own, so it's important that presenter Sara Demergi finds the ideal property that can help start the next chapter of Deborah's life.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien discover items in Wensleydale, North Yorkshire, that are ready for upcycling to turn a profit for their owners.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien assess sewing machines in Kent as they continue their journey across the UK to find unique and valuable items 'lost' in people's homes.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Debbie and Nigel start landscaping their five-acre grounds and risk a flat-pack fall out. Sarah and George run into trouble with the chateau's electrics as they host their first-ever wedding. Dick gets to work re-levelling the chateau's long driveway and Stephanie turns her attention to a new, modern apartment in the chateau's old stables.(n)

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton visits rural Cambridgeshire, where Darren and Luisa are building a picture-perfect waterfront home. However, problem after problem threatens to scupper the build and the couple struggle to delegate. Charlie has to deliver some hard home truths to ensure the project doesn't meander forever or leave the couple bankrupt and with nowhere to live.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at the Swan Inn in Pontfadog, a village in the Ceiriog Valley in Wrexham. Ian and Cathy McNeill take their guests trout fishing, and round the day off with a meal in their own restaurant with entertainment from a traditional tenor. However, the next morning is not so harmonious as the guests jostle for access to the only available shower.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition moves to Bournemouth, where owners John and Chris Griffith welcome their guests to the Bed & Breakfast by the Beach. The visit gets off to a bad start when hyper-critical Jill is reduced to tears by the discovery that her room does not have a smoke alarm, and the tension continues to mount at dinner, where the hosts regale their guests with racy tales.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition heads to the Lansbury in Whitby, North Yorkshire. All the guests are keen to have a good look around their rooms after falling victim to owner Jill Winspear's criticism earlier in the week - but her attempts at a guided tour backfire, and the breakfast service fails to impress.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The group's final destination is Charlie and Angie Hobson's Castle House in Denbigh, north Wales. Some of the guests are unimpressed by a day trip to a local spa - but back at the B&B, the previously critical Jill is full of praise.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The four sets of owners discover what the other competitors thought of their businesses by opening the cash-filled envelopes, having put in what they believed their hosts should receive. They meet for one last time to learn who has been declared the best value B&B of the week.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole calls in a locksmith to bring a safe back from the scrapheap, and a ship's wheel is left in splinters when the presenter falls out with restorer Guy.(n)

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Veteran actors Timothy West and Prunella Scales head to Llangollen, Denbighshire, where they spent their honeymoon. Reminiscing over how their relationship has survived and thrived over the past 50 years, the pair take in the picturesque sights of the Vale of Llangollen and the Montgomery Canal, before travelling on a horse-drawn barge up the Horseshoe Falls.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Phillip aims to transform three derelict rooms at Chateau de la Fare into the perfect bridal suite for his daughter Penny's wedding in three months' time.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets actor Sean Simons, who has bought the ruins of Cloontykilla Castle in Co Roscommon with a view to using them to create a spectacular mock 16th-century home. However, the outlandish plans - including Jacuzzis in the battlements and water-spouting gargoyles - have Kevin worried the project could become a garish nightmare, and his scepticism increases when he realises Sean has not consulted an architect. Disaster seems to be looming as the contractors grow frustrated and planning regulations are ignored, but somehow things slowly begin to take shape - until a national economic crisis plunges everything into chaos.(n)

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton is in Hampshire to meet Nikki and Darren, who have struck gold with their stunning seaside plot on which they want to build modern family home.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: Peter Maddison follows Barbara and Craig who have decided to build their dream home in Dee Why in New South Wales.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: An 84-year-old is rushed into hospital after losing control of his car and crashing into a road sign, and is in need of an emergency transfusion after his blood pressure drops dangerously low. programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and James May take a look at increasingly popular tall hatchbacks known as crossovers. Having concluded their primary appeal is to caravanners, the duo spend a couple of days in models by Mazda and Volkswagen and try to get a feel for what life is like for the people they believe would buy one. Meanwhile, Richard Hammond drives a pair of exciting Lamborghinis in Italy - the ferocious Aventador Roadster and the limited-edition Sesto Elemento, which carries an eye-watering price tag of around £2million. Two cars are also put through their paces on the test track, with James in a modern re-creation of the classic Porsche 911 and Aerosmith frontman Steven Tyler in the rather less exotic Reasonably Priced Car.(n)

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Vigilant Customs and Border Protection officers uncover 5,000 rounds of ammunition being smuggled across Hidalgo Bridge into Mexico.(n)

This time, they take on the iconic Lamborghini Huracan Performante in a handling head to head. Their mechanical ingenuity and driving skills are put to the ultimate test as they race against both the supercar and the clock.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson drives the Jaguar F-Type sports car on some of Britain's finest roads, while Richard Hammond jumps inside the Range Rover Sport and gives it a thorough workout off-road, on a track and in what he believes is its most natural habitat - the lanes of Cheshire. James May goes for a countryside adventure in the New Routemaster London bus brought into use in 2013, which is propelled by a turbodiesel generator and a powerful electric motor. Inspired by the machines they have tested, the trio then meet to celebrate Britain's motor industry with a grand finale in front of Buckingham Palace. Plus, former Formula One driver Mark Webber takes to the track for a lap in the Reasonably Priced Car.(n)

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson explores the history of the ancient kingdom on a walk from Melrose in the Scottish Borders to Holy Island off the coast of Northumberland. Over five days, he goes on the trail of one of Northumbria's greatest sons - Saint Cuthbert. Through the Tweed Valley and across the Cheviot Hills, it's a journey through a murky history laden with myth. However, by investigating the communities, power bases and landscape of seventh-century Northumbria, Tony aims to understand the pagan land in which Cuthbert spread his simple brand of Celtic Christianity.(n)

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson travels along the north Norfolk coast through one of the least developed spots in Britain, finding out how the area maintained its rural, remote identity despite the dynamic industrial changes of the Victorian age. From the arrival of Bertie, Prince of Wales at Sandringham House to the opening of Cromer Pier, Tony's four-day walk sees him learn about key periods in the county's history. He explores prime shooting estates, purpose-built seaside resorts, salt marshes and disused railway lines as he discovers how powerful landowners were able to control development and even shape the railways to suit their needs.(n)

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston tells the story of Hitler's state executioner, who was placed in charge of killing thousands of people declared enemies of the state.(n)

programme.title: Nazi Hunters

programme.title: Secrets of the Railways
programme.sub-title: The Tablelands Railways Line - Jungle Secrets
programme.desc: The Tablelands Railway Line in Queensland, Australia, which changed the face of one of the country's great states when it was constructed in the late 1800s.(n)

programme.title: Impossible Railways
programme.desc: Unusual rail networks designed to traverse water, including the Indian Ocean, the earthquake zones of the Bosphorus Strait and the sea cliffs of the Italian Riviera.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: An advanced German battleship that sank in a Norwegian fjord during the Second World War, the Miles Glacier Bridge in Alaska, and a mysterious concrete giant in Bulgaria.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow

programme.title: Mountain: Life at the Extreme
programme.desc: This episode looks at life on the highest mountain range on Earth, the Himalayas, home to many extraordinary animals and remarkable ancient cultures. The programme follows snow leopards as they creep into isolated mountain villages searching for food, and monkeys huddling for warmth in a hidden, frozen forest, and the athletes who compete in the gruelling Everest marathon.(n)

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough presents an insight into the most successful animals on the planet. Observing an Arctic fox stalk prey in its -20C environment, he reveals warm-bloodedness as one of the key factors in the mammals' global domination. He also studies marsupials in Australia and Central America, offering a unique view inside a platypus's breeding burrow.(n)

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson embarks on another expedition through some of Britain's most historic landscapes. The moors and valleys of West Yorkshire were the home of, and inspiration for, the Brontes, the literary family who produced such classics as Wuthering Heights and Jane Eyre. Over four days, Tony heads out from the Victorian wool capital of Bradford and treks in a giant loop around what is now known as Bronte Country. From the birth of Charlotte, Emily, Anne and their brother Branwell in the suburb of Thornton, he traces their childhoods to the much-romanticised Bronte hub of Haworth, finding that the harsh reality of life in the 1820s was far removed from the fantasy world dreamed up by the children at their father's parsonage home.(n)

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.sub-title: Walking Through History: Victoria and Albert's Highland Fling
programme.desc: Tony Robinson visits the Cairngorms National Park to discover how Queen Victoria and Prince Albert helped shape Scottish traditions and imagery, such as tartan, bagpipes and caber-tossing. Starting in Pitlochry, he traverses the Killiecrankie Pass, where the Jacobites won the Battle of Killiecrankie, and ends his trek at Balmoral and the fantasy castle Albert built as his and Victoria's Highland escape.(n)

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.sub-title: Walking Through History: Norman Conquest of Pembrokeshire
programme.desc: Tony Robinson heads to the south-west corner of Wales and one of Britain's finest coastal paths to find out why Pembrokeshire feels so English, even though it's more than 100 miles from the border. With its numerous castles and a fine cathedral, there are sure signs the Normans stamped their mark over the area. Tony's four-day walk traces the story of their conquest of the county, which he discovers was very different from the knockout victory at Hastings, involving a long, fiercely fought struggle.(n)

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.sub-title: Walking Through History: Nazi Occupation - Channel Islands
programme.desc: Tony Robinson heads to Guernsey and Jersey to tell the story of the islands' surrender to the Nazis during the Second World War. In Guernsey, he hears of the first bombing raid that heralded the German troops' arrival and sees the spot where the first-ever British commando landed in secret, only to see the planned revenge raid fail spectacularly. A ferry then takes him on to Jersey, where he finds almost all of its beaches have a bunker or a machine gun post left over from the conflict.(n)

Jamie rustles up a colourful pea falafel fritter wrap, complete with home-made hummus, crispy chickpeas and epic garnishes, while Jimmy takes the idea of movie night to the next level by building a popcorn maker. The boys also report on the increasing numbers of people choosing to replace dairy milk with plant-based alternatives.(n)
